## Learning to Serve Lord Krishna Proactively | Sri Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa | SB 3.2.15 | 27.11.2025
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

### devotees vs. non-devotees, divine compassion, Krishna's appearance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5k_6DDYzMgs?t=44
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When such friction takes place, the Lord, out of His great compassion, towards His pure devotees, appears in person, accompanied by His plenary portions, controlling the Mahat Tattva."
**Summary:** This segment explains how the Lord appears out of compassion for His devotees when they are harassed by non-devotees, comparing it to electricity generated by friction, and clarifying Krishna's position as the original Supreme Personality of Godhead.

Translator translation. But when there is friction between his peaceful devotees and persons who are in the material modes of nature, he takes birth just like fire, accompanied by the Mahat-Tattva. The devotees of the Lord are by nature peaceful because they have no material hankering. A liberated soul has no hankering and therefore he has no lamentation. One who wants to possess also laments when he loses his possession. Devotees have no hankering for material possessions and no hankering for spiritual salvation. They are situated in the transcendental loving service of the Lord as a matter of duty, and they do not mind where they are or how they have to act. Karmis, Jnanis and Yogis all hanker to possess some material or spiritual assets. Karmis want material possessions, Jnanis and Yogis want spiritual possessions, but devotees do not want any material or spiritual assets. They want only to serve the Lord anywhere in the material or spiritual worlds that the Lord desires and the Lord is always specifically compassionate towards such devotees. The Karmis, Jnanis and Yogis have their particular mentalities in the modes of nature and therefore they are called Itara or non-devotees. These Itaras, including even the Yogis, sometimes harass the devotees of the Lord. Durvasamani, a great Yogi, harassed Maharaj Ambarisha because the latter was a great devotee of the Lord. And the great Karmis and Jnanis, Hiranyakashipu, even harassed his own Vaishnava son, Prahlad Maharaj. There are many instances of such harassment of the peaceful devotees of the Lord by the Itaras. When such friction takes place, the Lord, out of His great compassion, towards His pure devotees, appears in person, accompanied by His plenary portions, controlling the Mahat Tattva. The Lord is everywhere in both the material and spiritual domains. And He appears for the sake of His devotees when there is friction between His devotee and the non-devotees. As electricity is generated by friction of matter anywhere and everywhere, the Lord being all-pervading appears because of the friction of devotees and non-devotees. When Lord Krishna appears on a mission, all His plenary portions accompany Him. When He appeared as the son of Vasudeva, there were differences of opinion about His incarnation. Some said He is the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Some said He is an incarnation of Narayana. And others said He is the incarnation of Kshirodakshayi Vishnu. But actually He is the original Supreme Personality of Godhead, Krishnastu Bhagawan Swayam. And Narayana, the Purusha, and all other incarnations accompany Him to function as different parts of His pastimes. Mahadamshayuktaha indicates that He is accompanied by the Purushas and who create the Mahat-Tattva, Mahat-Tanva. It is confirmed in the Vedic hints Mahantam Vibhum Atmanam. Lord Krishna appeared just like electricity when there was friction between Kamsa and Vasudeva and Ugrasena. Vasudeva and Ugrasena were the Lord's devotees and Kamsa, a representative of the Karmis and Jnanis, was a non-devotee. Krishna as He is, is compared to the sun. He first appeared from the ocean of the womb of Devaki and gradually satisfied the inhabitants of the places surrounding Mathura. Just as the sun enlivens the lotus flower in the morning, after gradually rising to the meridian of Dwaraka, the Lord set like the sun, placing everything in darkness, as described by Uddhava. This ends the Bhaktivedanta Purport.

---
### compassion, understanding God, preaching
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5k_6DDYzMgs?t=295
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "God gives one more chance, and there is next life wherein we have an opportunity to correct ourselves and then progress further in our spiritual life in the progressive life this makes sense."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates an encounter with a Christian doctor who questions the concept of eternal hell, highlighting the contrast with the Vaishnava understanding of God's compassion and the opportunity for spiritual progress through reincarnation, ultimately leading the doctor to appreciate the Hare Krishna mantra.

So this is a discussion between Vidura and Uddhava. Vidura asks about Krishna and his pastimes and the chapter is known as Remembrance of Lord Krishna by Uddhava. I think long ago I happened to visit one, a homeopathy doctor in our location. Both the hills, Banjara hills and Jubilee hills put together is a popular doctor. All cinema actors also goes to him. So then, when I first time I went there I saw the entire clinic is devoid of any pictures of God. So I am already guessing that there is something, you know, he is not into our line. And when I met him, I understood, of course, he is a Christian, but highly skilled doctor. So, you know, after the regular routine discussions, in the end, as usual, our Sampradaya, we, I gave some Prasadam box and I also left one of our magazines, Telugu magazine, with him, I came off. After one or two months, again I went, you know, just to give him the input, feedback on his first recommendations. So then, again we had a regular routine discussions about the health and all that. And, I was about to come off, then he asked me one question, Swamiji, can I ask you something? I said yes. He says, I am a, you know, my parents, they were converted to Christianity, and then I am kind of a second generation, you know, Christian in my, in my family. And, according to what I learnt, they always tell me that, if we commit mistakes, then, we will be thrown into hell. And so, do you believe in hell, he asked. So then I said, yes, hell and heaven are there. And then he says, no, my question is, they explain that there is a, in the hell, there is a big kadhai, huge kadhai, and there is a boiling oil inside. And then, the people who are thrown into the hell, they are all put into this kadhai, and boiling oil. So I was just listening to him. He asked me one interesting question, do you think there is day and night in hell? I said yes, there is. Then he said, Swamiji, okay, somebody has done a mistake, and God has thrown him into the hell, and he is boiling in the kadhai, and then that boiling oil, morning and night, morning and evening and night, day after day, boiling, boiling, boiling, what is the purpose of God in this, he asked me. Okay, one day, two day boiling, he understood, oh, it's very dangerous. Then after that, he has a next life. That's fine. But according to their theory, hell is eternal. Once somebody goes to hell, permanent. This is the only life, after this life, either you will go to hell, or you will go to heaven. This is their theory. So he is not able to understand. Let us say somebody goes to hell, day after day, day after day, day after day, boiling, boiling, and that too for eternity, what is the purpose? I asked him, do you see any purpose, I asked. No, Swamiji, I don't, it doesn't make sense to me. So I asked him, why it is so? He said, I don't know, I don't know, I don't know, I don't know, I don't know, I don't know, then I asked him why is it not making sense to you? He says, our own scripture says, that God is very compassionate, but when I am seeing that when his child, does some mistake, he is thrown into eternal hell, it doesn't make sense to me. And then he also said, even Bhagavad Gita makes sense Swamiji. God gives one more chance, and there is next life and wherein we have an opportunity to correct ourselves and then progress further in our spiritual life in the progressive life this makes sense and in the end he said I little bit read your Telugu book although I don't understand so much and then he said I liked that mantra you are Hare Krishna fortunately he started chanting some few times probably so this is the highly contradictory picture of God in many religions he is depicted as a very cruel dictator somebody who throws he carefully monitors everybody every smallest mistake and then in the end them into the hell here it is mentioned.

Today if I am powerful, smart, intelligent, I would like to become on my own and another leader. What is the lesson Hanuman is teaching? Hanuman is teaching that when you have a leader like Lord Ram, who is so glorious, who is so great, because of his virtues and values, people love to be their servant. Today many leaders, they think that they need loyal, committed and contributing followers and servants. But they need to ask question about their own self. Am I being a worthy leader like Lord Ram to attract a worthy follower, a worthy servant like Hanuman? The moment I am not a worthy leader like Lord Ram, who cared, who loved his followers. Because of that, the followers were ready to offer their lives in the service of Lord Shri Ram in the war with Ravan. That is because Ram was such an inspiring leader, who set personal examples, taking care of his own followers and following the virtues of Lord Ram. And he had great values and values in his personal life. That is why Lord Shri Ram, who is a great leader, attracted great servants, great followers like Hanuman.

## Lord Rama: An Ideal Leader | Lessons from Lord Rama | Sri Sampati Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

### leadership, integrity, truthfulness, promise
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VxNMQHkcFBw?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Lord Ram teaches the importance of being truthful and honoring the promise and being respectful towards our own words."
**Summary:** The lecture emphasizes Lord Rama as the ideal leader due to his unwavering commitment to his promises, highlighting the importance of integrity and truthfulness for effective leadership and building trust.

We cannot think of a leader who does not keep up his own words. You must have a virtue, a value called integrity. The followers will question the credibility of your leadership. Ram is the greatest leader. He went through tribulation just to keep up his promise. A leader is the one who builds immense trust in the heart of the followers. We cannot think of a leader who does not keep up his own words, who breaks the promises, right? We do not definitely like to choose such a leader. Ram is the greatest leader for the followers. He is the greatest leader for the entire human civilization because he took immense trouble, he went through tribulation just to keep up his promise, his family promise, his father's promise by giving away Ayodhya to his stepmother Kaikeyi and making Bharat as a king of Ayodhya. Ram could have easily become the king of Ayodhya, but he wanted to honor the words of his father Dasharatha Maharaj. For that, he happily gave up Ayodhya and went to forest. Lord Ram teaches the importance of being truthful and honoring the promise and being respectful towards our own words. The moment the leader makes a promise, he must make all efforts to keep up his promise. Otherwise, the followers will question the credibility of your leadership. They may lose faith in your leadership because you say something, but you are not intending to follow your own promise. So Lord Ram is teaching a very important lesson to all the leaders, saying that no matter how difficult it is, no matter how troublesome in your own personal life, you must keep up your words. You must have a virtue, a value called integrity. There must be absolutely no gap between your action and your words. You must always walk the talk. And Lord Ram is an example for somebody who always walked what he talked. So that is the lesson Lord Ram teaches as a hero, as a leader to all the aspiring leaders in human civilization.

---

## Material Solutions Alone Cannot Truly Uplift the World | SB 3.25.6-7 | Sri Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

### sense gratification, detachment, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2I5lPlBUOIU?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Unless one becomes tired of material sense gratification, there is no opportunity to hear transcendental messages from a person like Kapila."
**Summary:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa reads and explains Srimad Bhagavatam 3.25.6-7, focusing on Devahuti's frustration with material senses and her desire for relief through hearing from Lord Kapila.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, 3rd Canto, Chapter 25, The Glories of Devotional Service, Texts 6 and 7. 6 has no purport, so I will read it by myself. Tamasinam Akarmanam Tatvamarga Gradarshanam Svajutam Devahutyaha Dhatusam Smriti Vachaha. Translation: When Kapila, who could show her the ultimate goal of the Absolute Truth, was sitting leisurely before her, Devahuty remembered the words Brahma had spoken to her, and she therefore began to question Kapila as follows. Text 7. Yena Sambhavya Manena Prapannandham Tamav Prabho. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace, A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami, Srila Prabhupada Ki Jai. Translation: Devahuty said, I am very sick of the disturbance caused by my material senses, for because of this sense disturbance, my Lord, I have fallen into the abyss of ignorance. Here the word Asad-Indriya-Tarshanaat is significant. Asad means impermanent, temporary, and Indriya means senses. Thus, Asad-Indriya-Tarshanaat means from being agitated by the temporarily manifest senses of the material body. We are evolving through different statuses of material bodily existence, sometimes in a human body, sometimes in an animal body. Animal body and therefore the engagements of our material senses are also changing. Anything which changes is called temporary or asat. We should know that beyond these temporary senses are our permanent senses which are now covered by the material body. The permanent senses being contaminated by matter are not acting properly. Devotional service therefore involves freeing the senses from this contamination. When the contamination is completely removed and the senses act in the purity of unalloyed Krishna consciousness, we have reached sadindriya or eternal sensory activities. Eternal sensory activities are called devotional service whereas temporary sensory activities are called sense gratification. Unless one becomes tired of material sense gratification, there is no opportunity to hear transcendental messages from a person like Kapila. Devahuti expressed that she was tired. Now that her husband had left home, she wanted to get relief by hearing the instructions of Lord Kapila.

---
### senses, agitation, ignorance, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2I5lPlBUOIU?t=185
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I am very disturbed by asadindriyatarsana, the agitations of my senses, and the senses themselves are Asat."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the context of the verses, explaining Devahuti's appeal to Lord Kapila due to her distress from the agitation of the impermanent senses and her subsequent surrender.

So in these verses we are seeing the context of Kapila Muni leisurely sitting in his hermitage and the mother was seated before him. She recollected Brahma's words that her son Kapila is actually an incarnation of Vishnu. So she is now appealing to Kapila, Lord Kapila. Nirvinna Nitaram Bhuman. Bhuman means O my Lord, I'm very disturbed by asadindriyatarsana. The agitations of my senses, and the senses themselves are Asat. Asat means impermanent. After all the body means Asat it has time period to die. Certainly the senses also will be Asat. So asadindriyatarsana means the body dying. These terms mean asad asatten1 or versión que es breeds to die of all whether the body dies. Assatan that too means asiata. therefore i prostrate from the body, meaning to live when the body dies. Asada means impermanent. after all the body is when he dies of it and the body is serving his consent ducts , by performing all these steps together this process will be assisted. means from the pulling or the agitation of the senses. And she is saying, nirvinna netaram, I am very very disgusted with all this, ena sambhavyamanena prapannam. So she is saying, I am very sick of the disturbance caused by my material senses. Because of this disturbance, andham tamaprabhu, I have fallen into tamaha andha, very abyss of ignorance, andham suprapanna. So she is saying, I have fallen into this kind of an abyss of ignorance, now I am surrendering unto you. So this is the beginning of the conversation between Devahoti and the Lord Kapila.

The chapter is titled as Glories of Devotional Service. The legs are there. tongue is there so there are five senses which are for collecting information knowledge acquiring there are five more senses for working so these 10 senses are always seeking engagements senses don't sit idly always pulls it always seeks engagements this so one one sense is pulling the person in one one direction eyes are pulling us towards beautiful persons or beautiful things in this world ears are pulling us into good music interesting music however nonsense it may be it is very nice to hear so ears are pulling us towards that and then nostrils are seeking good smells wonderful smells and then the tongue is seeking good taste and then the tongue is seeking good taste and then the tongue is seeking good taste the skin is wanting to touch the soft things like that each of these senses are pulling the person in one direction.

### tongue, sense control, devotee, human life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2I5lPlBUOIU?t=472
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Human life begins when one gets frustrated with this satisfaction of the senses."
**Summary:** The speaker identifies the tongue as the strongest sense and emphasizes the difficulty in controlling it. He highlights that only pure devotees are free from sensory disturbances and that Devahuti presents herself as an ordinary person appealing for help. He states that human life begins when one becomes frustrated with sense gratification.

Out of all these senses surprisingly the strongest the most formidable sense is the tongue controlling the tongue tongue or handling this tongue is most forbidden. So this indriyatarshana, these disturbances are there for everybody. Nobody is free from this except a pure devotee. So here Devahuti presenting herself as an ordinary person, she has appealed to Lord Kapila that I am indriyatarshana, this is disturbing me. So when the disturbances come in life because of the senses or whatever way, she is immediately approaching a higher personality who can give knowledge to her, who can give a way out. Agitation could be asiddhindriyatarshana, it could be because of mind, it could be because of intelligence, whatever, the agitations are there. Then one has to approach Guru, the spiritual master for relief. And when you approach you have to say prapanna, I surrender unto you. One has to approach the spiritual master with the mood of surrenderance. So in this world everybody is embarrassed by this asiddhindriyatarshana, everybody is embarrassed. So, Prabhupada says, human life begins when one gets frustrated with this satisfaction of the senses. Asadhindriyatarshana.

### sense gratification, spiritual body, material body, duality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2I5lPlBUOIU?t=620
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The basic philosophy is that we are not this body, we are a spirit soul."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts human life with animal life, stating that animals are comfortable with sense gratification, while humans become disgusted with it. He emphasizes the basic philosophy that we are not the material body but a spirit soul with a spiritual body and senses covered by the material body, creating a duality.

As long as one is not frustrated or disgusted, he is leading an animal life. Animals are most comfortable in sense gratification. But a human being means, you know, here we see Devahuti, Nirvindanatha Ram, disgusted with this. So this appears to be very surprising to materialists, because they always chase, one after another, satisfaction of the senses. They always chase. And they are so busy in chasing objects for, or victims for, satisfying their senses, that they are completely busy throughout the day. This is the scenario that we see. And what is the process to reduce these disturbance caused by the senses? Here Prabhupada is saying, devotional service, therefore, involves freeing the senses from the contamination. The senses are currently contaminated by matter. We have to understand this basic philosophy. Otherwise, we don't understand these concepts at all. The basic philosophy is that we are not this body, we are a spirit soul. Spirit soul has spiritual body. Spirit soul has spiritual senses. But those senses are covered by this body. The spiritual body is covered by this material body. That is the duality in this world. What is the duality? The basic reason is this. We are originally having a spiritual body, but we are caught up in this material body. So, the material bodies are covering the original senses. So, when you try to enjoy, you are not able to enjoy the original senses.

---
### senses, material body, devotional service, false senses
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2I5lPlBUOIU?t=752
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This body is actually a covering."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of a gulab jamun and a tongue covered by a bandage, the speaker illustrates how the material body covers our original, spiritual senses, preventing us from experiencing true pleasure. He emphasizes that devotional service is the means to get rid of this material influence.

You are trying to enjoy the material senses which are false. For example, let us say you want to enjoy one gulab jamun. And you put it on the tongue and immediately you will see the juicy, all the pleasure sensations. Let us say some disease has come and the tongue is covered by one bandage. The doctor has put one bandage, let us say. And then you put rasgulla or gulab jamun. Same gulab jamun. You are covered. The tongue is covered by something else. So, you don't get that original pleasure. Similarly, we have original senses. Spiritual senses are there, but they are covered by this body. Bandage. This body is actually a covering. The body itself is a problem. Covering. So, this has to be recognized. So, how do we get rid of this matter, material influence? That is devotional service. Prabhupada is explaining here devotional service. Prabhupada is saying the permanent senses being contaminated by matter are not acting properly. The senses are there, but now currently they are covered by this matter. So, they are not acting properly. Original senses are covered. So, that's why what we are all enjoying is asadindriya, false, temporary senses. They are not our original senses. So much time, energy, everything we are investing, but what are we enjoying? It's only asadindriya, tarshana. False senses.

---
### asat, Krishna's pleasure, devotional service, tongue control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2I5lPlBUOIU?t=884
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When the senses are engaged in Krishna's pleasure, that is called devotional service."
**Summary:** The speaker defines 'asat' as anything temporary or changing and explains that the engagements of the senses change life after life. He states that devotional service is engaging the senses in Krishna's pleasure and that this gradually reduces contamination. He emphasizes the importance of controlling the tongue by chanting Hare Krishna and eating Krishna Prasad.

And Prabhupada explains what is this asat. Asat means anything which is temporary or anything which is changing. This is asat. When somebody is in an animal form, his senses work in certain way. In human form, works in certain way. So, the engagements of the senses are changing. Life after life, life after life, are within the same life. Our engagements are constantly changing. That's why they are known as asat. Asatindriya, tarshana. So, when the senses are engaged in Krishna's pleasure, that is called devotional service. And as we go on engaging our senses in Krishna's service without any motivations, without any expectations, then the contamination and the disturbance slowly comes down. Asatindriya, tarshana. The disturbances will come down. This is done. Tushikena rushikesha-sevanam bhaktir ucyate. The definition is given. What is the definition of bhakti? Engaging the senses in the service of the master of the senses. Rushikesha. Rushika means senses. Engaging them in the service of the master of the senses. And that is who is Rushikesha? That is Krishna. So, as soon as we start engaging the senses in the service of Rushikesha, then our devotional service begins. And what is the first, the most difficult sense is tongue. So, that's why our bhakti starts by engaging the tongue in the service of the Supreme. And what is the tongue's function? You can produce some sound. Okay, chant Hare Krishna. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Whenever you feel like talking, producing some sound, don't produce nonsense. Chant Hare Krishna. And then whenever you want to eat something, then don't eat nonsense. Eat Krishna Prasad. If somebody can do this, what a tremendous progress. That is devotional service.

---
### Upadi, devotional service, identity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2I5lPlBUOIU?t=1038
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The biggest enemy for engaging the senses in the service of the Lord is Upadi."
**Summary:** The speaker identifies Upadi (designations) as the biggest enemy in engaging the senses in devotional service. He lists examples of Upadis like nationality, religion, and gender, and emphasizes that identifying with these designations diverts sensual engagements away from Krishna.

So, when you engage like that, the contamination will go away. The original sense also gets activated. There is no difference between this temporary sense and the original sense. That's activated. The biggest enemy for engaging the senses in the service of the Lord is Upadi. Sarvopadi Vinurimuktham Tat Paratvena Nirmalam Rushikesha Sevanam Bhaktiruttama If you want to engage the senses, what is the greatest enemy? It is Upadi. And what are these Upadis? I am a male. I am a male. I am an Indian. I am a Pakistani. I am a Hindu. I am a Christian. I am a Muslim. I am this. I am that. I am this. But who is thinking I am eternal servant of Krishna? That Upadi is our original Upadi. That we are not bothered. So, as soon as we think that we are, we are so-and-so, so-and-so, so-and-so, our sensual engagements will be diverted towards that Upadi. We develop emotions. We develop activities. Everything is centered around that Upadi. Let us say there is a Bhagavatam book. The icon is there on your mobile. That is an app, Bhagavatam. And next to it is the regular browser where you can see the regular mundane news. And let us say you are influenced by an Upadi. What will you click? You want to see what's happening between this and between that. Politically, nationally, in so many ways we get influenced. Because Upadis are controlling us. We take pleasure. As in, you know, as in this thing, as in that. Some designation. I don't want to mention.

In the air. Pakistan fellow hits six. Where? In the ground. Why is that? What is acting here? Upadi. I am identifying myself as an Indian soul. I get elated when something comes in favour. And then I get distressed when something goes against. So this is the scenario. So our process is Sarvopadi Nirmuktam. We have to get rid of all these Upadis. And attain our natural position as an eternal being. Our position as an eternal servant of Krishna. So that's why we should know the biggest enemy to engage in our devotional service to Krishna is these Upadis. So then you may say, so that's the broad level goal that we all have to have. An eternal servant of Krishna. How he has to behave. How he has to engage all these disturbances in Krishna's service. That is Guru. The spiritual master will guide us. There will be lot of disturbances will come in our life. And we are not fully pure from this Upadi's influence. So there will be agitation. There will be disturbances. Now how to handle all these things? That is Guru. Spiritual master will guide us. And in this world you will see, this world is a place of envy and hatred. Hatred. So much hatred will be all around us. This is the nature of this world. It is a very envious place. The envy begins with the envy of the Supreme Lord Krishna. And that will be reflected in innumerable ways. We envy other Jivas. We envy other human beings. We envy other area people. We envy other colour people. We envy other religion people. So many ways we can envy.

Only beating the legs. Immediately Pariksit Maharaj confronts him. He takes out his sword and says, I am going to finish you off now. Now, because in my kingdom, in this kingdom where Pandavas have ruled, my forefathers have ruled, that prestige I cannot allow you to destroy. Pariksit Maharaj is very concerned about the prestige of his forefathers and how this man is trying to disturb this. Immediately. Immediately he wants to finish him off. Just beating the cows. There is a credibility. We know. Peacefully we can execute our Krishna consciousness.

## Nurturing the Future: Important Responsibility for the Youth ISri Satya Gaurachandra Dasa ISB 6.5.36
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

### Narada Muni, Daksha, Renunciation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3_Vjx52LTZo?t=0
**Verse References:** SB 6.5.36
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "They must show everyone the path of renouncing this material world and returning home back to Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker reads and explains Srimad Bhagavatam 6.5.36, focusing on Daksha's criticism of Narada Muni for encouraging his sons to take the path of renunciation.

OM NAMO BHAGAVATE VASUDEVAYA. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 6, Chapter 5, Text 36. SRI DHAKSHA VACHA. AHO ASADO SADHUNAM SADHULINGEN NASTVAYA ASADVAKAR ADBHAKANAM VIKSHO MARGA PRADARSHITAHA. Translation purported by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swamishloka Upad. Translation: Prajapati Daksha said, "Alas, Narada Muni, you wear the dress of a saintly person, but you are not actually a saint. Indeed, although I am now in Guru Hasto life, I am a saintly person. By showing my sons the path of renunciation, you have done me an abominable injustice." Purported by Eshra Prabhupada. Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu said Sanyasira Alpachidra Sarvaloke Gaye. In society, one will find many sanyasis, vanaprastas, grohastas and brahmacharis, but if all of them properly live in accordance with their duties, they are understood to be sadhus. Prajapati Daksha was certainly a sadhu because he had executed such great austerities that the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Vishnu, had appeared before him. Nevertheless, he had a fault-finding mentality. He improperly thought Narada Muni to be a sadhu. Narada Muni, however, taking advantage of their highly elevated position in austerity, instructed them to become Vaishnavas in the renounced order. This is the duty of Narada Muni and his followers. They must show everyone the path of renouncing this material world and returning home back to Godhead. Prajapati Daksha, however, could not see the exaltedness of the duties Narada Muni performed in relation to his sons. Unable to appreciate Narada Muni's behavior, Daksha accused Narada of being a sadhu. 

---
### Varnashrama, Renunciation, Krishna Consciousness Movement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3_Vjx52LTZo?t=170
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Generally, a Gruhasta thinks that unless one enters Gruhasta life, he cannot properly enter the renounced order."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the concept of renunciation, the roles within the varnashrama system, and draws parallels between Daksha's disapproval of Narada Muni and the modern-day resistance to the Krishna consciousness movement.

The words Bhikshur Margha, the path of renunciation, the measure of renunciation, are very insignificant in this regard. A sanyasi is called Tridhandi Bhikshu because his duty is to beg alms from the homes of gruhastas and to give them gruhastas' spiritual instructions. A sanyasi is allowed to beg from door to door, but a gruhasta cannot do so. Gruhastas may earn their living according to the four divisions of spiritual life. A Brahmana gruhasta may earn his livelihood by becoming an ascetic, learned scholar and teaching people in general how to worship the Supreme Personality of Godhead. He may also assume the duty of worship himself. Therefore it is said that only Brahmanas may engage in Deity worship and they may accept as Prasad whatever people offer the Deity. Although a Brahmana may sometimes accept charity, it is not for his personal maintenance but for the worship of the Deity. Thus a Brahmana does not stock anything for his future use. Similarly Kshatriyas may collect taxes from the citizens and they must also protect the citizens, enforce rules and regulations and maintain law and order. Vaishyas should earn their livelihood through agriculture and cow protection and Shudras should maintain their livelihood by serving the three higher classes. Unless one becomes a Brahmana and one cannot take Sanyas. Sanyasis and Brahmacharis may beg alms door-to-door but a Gruhasta cannot. Prajapati Daksha condemned Narada Muni because Narada, a Brahmachari who could beg from door-to-door, had made Sanyasis of Daksha's sons who were being trained to be Gruhastas. Daksha was extremely angry at Narada because he thought that Narada had done him a great injustice. According to Daksha, Daksha's opinion, Narada Muni had misled Daksha's inexperienced sons, Asadu, Akari or Bhakarnam. Daksha regarded his sons as innocent boys who had been misled when Narada showed them the renounced order of life. Because of all these considerations, Prajapati Daksha charged that Narada Muni was Asadu and should not have adopted the dress of a Sadhu. Sometimes a saintly person is misunderstood by Gruhastas, especially when he instructs their young sons to accept Krishna consciousness. Generally, a Gruhasta thinks that unless one enters Gruhasta life, he cannot properly enter the renounced order. If a young man immediately adopts the path of the renounced order in accordance with the instructions of Narada, or a member of his disciplic succession, his parents become very angry. The same phenomenon is occurring in our Krishna consciousness moment, because he is not a person who can accept this renounced order. We are instructing all the young boys in the western countries to follow the path of renunciation. We allow gruhastha life but a gruhastha also follows the path of renunciation. Even a gruhastha has to give up so many bad habits that his parents think his life has been practically destroyed. We allow no meat eating, no illicit sex, no gambling and no intoxication and consequently the parents wonder how if there are so many no's once life can be positive. In the western countries especially, these four prohibited activities practically constitute the life and soul of the modern population. Therefore parents sometimes dislike our movement just as Prajapati Daksha disliked the activities of Narada and accused Narada of dishonesty.

---
### Disciplic Succession, Parental Concerns, Renunciation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3_Vjx52LTZo?t=396
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "People addicted to householder life wonder how one can give up the enjoyment of gruhastha life which is a concession for sex enjoyment simply to become a mendicant in Krishna consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of following the disciplic succession from Narada Muni, addresses the concerns of parents regarding their children's involvement in Krishna consciousness, and highlights the Vedic injunction to renounce household life at a certain age.

Nevertheless, although parents may be angry at us, we must perform our duty without hesitation because we are in the disciplic succession from Narada. People addicted to householder life wonder how one can give up the enjoyment of gruhastha life which is a concession for sex enjoyment simply to become a mendicant in Krishna consciousness. They do not know that the householder's concession for sex life cannot be regulated unless one accepts the life of a mendicant. The Vedic civilization therefore enjoins that at the end of one's fiftieth year one must give up household life. This is compulsory. However, because modern civilization is misled, householders want to remain in family life until death and therefore they are suffering. In such cases, the disciples of Narada Muni advise all the members of the younger generation to join the Krishna consciousness movement immediately. There is nothing wrong in this. The Sanastha Bhaktivedanta Prabhupada. This is a thumping advice from Srila Prabhupada and a clear instruction that all members of the younger generation should join Krishna consciousness movement immediately. There is nothing wrong in this. So this is a wonderful instruction. Thank you. Thank you. and approving our folk activities to recruit from the younger generation to come and join this movement so that they don't follow the same path that their elders are trying to show. The problem with the modern world is that none of them are trained up in understanding what is the right path so although the elders, the parents and family members they are very protective about their children to see that they all follow you know a process that is approved by the society or according to their own standards. Everybody is concerned and they want their child to follow the right path. They want their child to be in a safe path. So while that is understandable, the point is that the parents themselves do not know what is that safe path. They themselves do not understand what is safe for any person. So when they are like that it becomes very difficult for the younger family members to take up the right path because the elders will always resist. So here the same phenomena we can see in this verse, the conversation between Daksha and Narada Muni. In this particular scene we can see Daksha very angry, in the last verse we have seen his lips are trembling, so you can imagine how angry he is and then he is looking at Narada Muni and he is saying Aho Asadhu, his immediate addressing of the Narada Muni is, you are a non-devotee, you are an Asadhu, Sadhunam Sadhulingena, you're wearing the dress of a Sadhu but actually by your activities we can easily see that you're actually not a Sadhu. What is his activity? Asadhu, Akhari, Arbha Kaana, my innocent sons, they don't know what is the world, they don't know what is what, they're all very innocent and you have convinced them to take a Bhikshor Marga, the path of holding a begging bowl, that is, that is Brahmachari's and Sanyasi's Bhikshor Marga Pradarshitaha, you are showing such a Marga to them, why is he upset? Because he is planning to train them up to become a very good Guru Ashtas who can produce children like him. So Daksha's situation can be understandable, initially he performed a lot of austerities, tapas, he saw Lord Vishnu

what happens is, the mother and father will become the first kind of gurus, their actions and activities will have great impact on the children, so the father and mother, you know, they behave only materialistically and materialistic path means, always quarrels will be there and if they are always quarreling with each other, the child is growing in an environment which is not very conducive for spiritual progress or rather any individualism. So, Daksha says, I am a saintly person, I am a saintly person, I am a saintly person, I am not a devotee. Daksha claiming himself to be a sadhu, is nothing wrong, but then is finding fault with Narada Munis activities. Why? if you see, Prabhupada has, our movement has five acres land there and that land belongs to the local government department, Indomit department, so they were thinking of giving you know Hare Krishna people a chance to develop that land and then renovate the temple and build something better, so when all those discussions are going on, there are many dynamics are working, that Indomit Commissioner, then he came to ISKCON Bangalore temple and at that time I think is Brahmosava, Jhulan also is going on, so obviously our devotees have ensured that he also does Jhula and when he's doing Jhula, he got a call and then he got a good news, that he got a promotion from Commissioner to Principal Secretary, just by doing Jhula so he took it very, I know very auspicious, became very favorable and then he went back and then things moved on and then, government has given that land on lease to Hare Krishna movement and then after many, after one or two years or so, I happened to meet him and then he was explaining about this visit, Iskcon Bangalore visit, I know that's this incident, that he got a promotion and all that, so I was hoping that he will tell me, that promotion this and that, that's how I was convinced, No. He said Swami ji, you know why I got convinced, he said, I said I mean I don't know you, you have to tell me. Then he said when I came to ISKCON Bangalore, I happened to go to your toilets. The way they are kept clean, I understood this is the organization I should give. MMD service got that land. Imagine MMD services, what a powerful preacher. That land is about 80 crores per acre. Ordinary LLP preachers, how much we can get? MMD got 400 crores. So each devotee is a preacher. We are preaching always. By decorating deities nicely, we are preaching. By keeping the floor clean, we are preaching. Nobody should feel that their service is inferior. Nor nobody should feel that their ashrama is inferior. As long as one is absorbed in this preaching and dedicating whatever he has, the time, energy and resources in Krishna's service, he should confidently, he will confidently progress. So that is our system.

Robot ki aisa aan k hai. This is referred to as by one of the chief personalities in the Kuru dynasty is asking questions to the muni pradhana. So after these inquiries, the conversation moves back to the conversation between. So here we see Shukadeva Goswami is explaining the reaction of after hearing the questions of what was the response of Maitreya Muni. He was thus questioned by Vidura. He knows how to explain, and then pravuruddha harsho became very enthusiastic. Means enhancement, extremely satisfied. So by hearing the questions of Vidura. Unleavened, very enthusiastic. Why? Because Bhagavat Kathayam, he got an opportunity to explain about the transcendental activities of the supreme personality of Godhead. Inspired by or questioned by or infused by Vidura and be very happy to explain these activities. So we will see in the next chapter Maitreya Rishi starts answering the inquiries of Vidura, and these answers are nothing but description about Purana Kalpaha. These are all descriptions from various Puranas from the Vedas. Maitreya Rishi answers the questions by Vidura.

### Bliss, Krishna, Association

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

Prabhupada explains that these Kathayams are full of bliss. There is transcendental bliss in hearing and explaining these activities of the Supreme Lord. Krishna is complete bliss, is complete knowledge, is pure knowledge, and he is permanent. So that is explained as such, Sat Chit and Ananda is a notion of bliss, and the activities that he performs, they are also completely blissful. It may be killing of a demon, or it could be dancing with happiness. So anybody who starts associating with these activities, any devotee who starts associating with these activities, associating with the Supreme Lord, how? By hearing and explaining, just by hearing and explaining, we become, we start associating with these Leelas, with these transcendental activities with Krishna. So.

### Preaching, Enthusiasm, Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AEyFGEPnuZ8?t=477
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Prabhupada never felt tired if there is a preaching opportunity; he has so much of energy."
**Summary:** This segment illustrates Prabhupada's unwavering enthusiasm for preaching, recounting an anecdote where he readily agreed to a late-night radio show despite extensive travel, demonstrating his dedication regardless of the audience size.

As the devotee goes on hearing and explaining, gradually he develops transcendental bliss, gradually he develops transcendental bliss, gradually he also enjoys explaining these stories, explaining these Kathas. Actually, we see this in Prabhupada's life. Whenever he used to go to any, you know, any place, devotees would organize various preaching programs, and then Prabhupada would get ready, and then he used to go to one preaching program after one more, continuously used. Like one devotee was recollecting Prabhupada travels, it's a long distance, he travels by flight, and he comes to that place, and he goes for some program to explain about Krishna, explain about transcendental activities of Krishna, Krishna Katha, and then there is one more program comes up at 11 o'clock in the night, and he was thinking, my God, Prabhupada traveled so much, already he would have been tired, so should I ask about this preaching opportunity or not. Finally, somehow he makes up his mind and approaches Prabhupada and says, Prabhupada, so there is a radio show at 11 o'clock. Prabhupada says ok, how many people attend this. Then he says there will be millions here listening to this. Prabhupada says let's go, and then he says, no no Prabhupada, you have come all the way, you have traveled, you are tired, you must be tired, you know, I don't want to take advantage of your presence here, and Prabhupada says don't worry, and then he goes to the program, radio program, and he addresses the audience. Actually, Prabhupada never felt tired if there is a preaching opportunity; he has so much of energy, he never felt tired, he used to go and preach to the people, and sometimes disciples will say, you know, thousands will come, hundreds will come, and then only some thirty forty will come, and sometimes twenty will come, but you see, Prabhupada never gets disappointed, he continues to preach whether it is two three people, whether it is hundreds and thousands in India.

### Enthusiasm, Spiritual Life, Service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AEyFGEPnuZ8?t=630
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without enthusiasm, there is no chance of spiritual life."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts Prabhupada's enthusiasm with the example of a successful businessman always seeking new opportunities, drawing a parallel to how devotees should eagerly embrace service opportunities.

Disciples organized this, this Pandal programs in Mumbai, in Hyderabad, thousands would come, daily twenty five thousand, thirty thousand people would come, and then when Prabhupada addresses the audience, there will be thousands sitting and listening. So Prabhupada has seen that, Prabhupada also seen where they thought hundred people will come, and you will see sometimes one person, two persons would come in the western countries. So, but pravruddha harsha, Prabhupada as you see, he is very enthusiastic. Actually, without enthusiasm, there is no chance of spiritual life. Krishna consciousness or spiritual life means one has to be very enthusiastic. Yes, I want to do this service, yes, I want to preach. Now what kind of an enthusiasm? Prabhupada gives an example of a business man earning money. So if you talk to a successful business person who is earning a lot of money, you go and ask him, see look, there is one more opportunity here, and then what will be the response of that business man? Immediately he will catch on to that opportunity, he will say yes let me go there, let me start one more branch, let me start one more business venture. That's what he is, he will not be thinking, no I am only one person, how can I do this, no need, it's ok, let us leave this opportunity, you can go to some other business man and give that opportunity, he will never say that, continuously he will be, you know, snatching those, lapping on to those opportunities. So.

### Devotee, Service, Vaishnavism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AEyFGEPnuZ8?t=750
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A devotee immediately latches on to doing that one more service."
**Summary:** This segment defines a devotee as someone who eagerly embraces additional service opportunities, contrasting this with reluctance and highlighting the Vaishnava tradition where the spiritual master appreciates the disciple for providing service opportunities.

Similarly, a devotee means he, he is, if some more service is been offered, one more service or one more. So a devotee immediately latches on to doing that one more service, not that so much service I am doing from the morning, and suddenly one more service has come to me. So, you know, how can I do this, no, one should be pravurdharsha, he becomes more enthusiastic. Yes, I want to do one more. There Radha Govinda Ki Jai. Sanchoddhaha, infused, here Vidura is asking question, so he is infusing that enthusiasm. So similarly somebody will come to you and say, do one more service, actually he is infusing one more opportunity he is infusing in your life, but generally we become very upset with him, see so much I am working, one more service, but here you see, he is very happy with Vidura. Actually, we will see his answers in the next shloka, Maitreya Muni glorifies Vidura, oh you are so nice, you have given me one more opportunity to talk on the transcendental activities of the Lord, you are so wonderful. You will see he glorifies Vidura first of all, although he is a spiritual master, Vidura is a disciple, but still he glorifies the disciple. You see this tradition only in Vaishnavism, in Vaishnava Sampradaya, a spiritual master congratulates and thanks the disciple because he has been given one more opportunity to serve Krishna.

### Humility, Service, Spiritual Energy

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Real humility means how I can preach more and more."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts genuine humility, which is expressed through eagerness to preach, with artificial humility, which manifests as reluctance, emphasizing that spiritual energy is gained through service and chanting.

This is the mood of the devotees. So here, sanchodita prahasan, he is not answering grudgingly, oh one more question, so many questions you have asked, hours have passed by, now one more question, some more questions, no, prahasan is actually smiling. He is not asking bring me one more plate of some prashad or something, already my mouth is dried up, how can I go on answering your question, he is not saying, he is very happy. So this is spiritual life, spiritual energy. If somebody is really absorbed in service, chanting and hearing, if he really hears, if he really serves nicely, actually he gets spiritual energy, he is working under the shelter of spiritual energy. Material energy means tiredness, boring, no, happiness, this is material energy, spiritual energy means devotees have to be very enthusiastic to preach. If there is an opportunity to preach, smiling, happy, and you should go and preach, yes, I am ready, one class after another class, he should not say, no no, I don't want to. Real humility means how I can preach more and more, this is real humility. Artificial humility means no no no I don't want to, I cannot, I cannot preach, this is artificial humility. Real humility means he is ready to preach more and more, what do I know to preach, I am, I am not very qualified, so let me not, no, humility means one should be.

Chaitanya Charita Amrita says such places should be taken as Prabhudatta Desha, the places that are assigned, allocated for me, the service that is allocated for me, I have to preach, I have to take that as Prabhudatta. Datta, in Telugu, Datta means you have adopted, you have adopted the place and to preach, not for enjoying, not for exploitation, but to preach. As long as anardhas are there in the mind, in the heart, sometimes we will be very enthusiastic and sometimes we will not be very enthusiastic, that ups and downs will be there, but then what is our inspiration to preach then? That Prabhudatta Desha, the inspiration is order of the spiritual master.

My guru has told me, so I have to do it, then you will get inspiration, then you will get all the. When will my mind be completely surrendered unto your lotus feet? This is the prayer to the spiritual master, what is that? When will my mind be completely surrendered unto your lotus feet? At that time, only by your mercy, I shall be able to get relief from all obstacles to spiritual life. Sometimes we feel there are too many obstacles, not able to move an inch, not able to proceed further, not able to progress, we feel like that. So Shridhara Swami is saying how we should pray to the spiritual master. So he is asking when will my mind be completely surrendered unto your lotus feet? At that time, by your mercy, all these obstacles will be relieved, removed from my life, and I shall be situated in blissful life. Now this is the secret to get bliss, what is that? ### Surrender, Preaching, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AEyFGEPnuZ8?t=1360
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When I see that one is engaged seriously and sincerely in discharging the duties ordered by the spiritual master, that person becomes most dear to me."
**Summary:** This segment explains that true surrender means actively preaching the message and fulfilling the spiritual master's orders, which pleases Krishna, who observes everyone's activities as the Supersoul.

What is the meaning? Yes one is to meditate on the lotus feet of the spiritual master, that is good, but really the surrenderance means how we can take this movement to as many people as possible. Real surrenderance means how I can preach this message. Prabhupada wanted me to do this. So if somebody takes the order of the spiritual master very seriously and try to execute that order as much as possible, then how will Krishna see this? If you try to take up guru's order, how will Krishna sees this? Now Krishna explains himself very interesting. He sees as the super soul of the living entities sitting in everyone's heart. Krishna is present in everyone's heart as the super soul, he is present. So he is saying as super soul, I observe everyone's activity in every stage and order of life, in every stage, we may be in deep trouble, we may be having lot of obstacles, and this and that, every stage. So Krishna is saying I observe everyone's activity regardless of which stage one is in. Now Krishna is saying you may be in whatever stage, regardless of whatever stage one is in, when I see that one is engaged seriously and sincerely in discharging the duties ordered by the spiritual master. Now Krishna is saying Krishna is observing not only devotees activities, he is observing everybody's activities, all living entities activities. Anumanta Upadrashta, he is an observer. 13th chapter he says that Shedrachnyam Chaapimam Viduram, the other day Maharaj is also mentioning this Cha, one little word, Sanskrit word that Cha means that he is also present along with the Jeevatma in the heart, along with the soul, super soul is also present and is observing the activities. So when he sees that somebody is engaged seriously and sincerely, these are the two qualifications, it doesn't matter what is the material qualification, whether he is educated or not educated, one has to be serious and sincere, then in discharging the duties ordered by the spiritual master, then what happens? And is thus dedicating his life.

To the service of the spiritual master, that person becomes most dear to me. Actually, only Krishna understands what is our real difficulty, nobody else will be able to understand. So those and he is saying whatever stage you may be in because Krishna understands that, but if you are enthusiastic to execute the order and dedicate your time and energy, dedicating life means your time and energy for executing this order of the spiritual master, then he says he becomes very dear. You want to become very dear to Krishna, this is the formula. The supreme personality of Godhead will be smiling on you if you try to execute the orders of the spiritual master, and for that we need to pray to Guru, offer our prayers. When will my mind be attached to your instructions, your orders, when will my mind be very focused on it, only at that time all my challenges will be removed from my life.

So and then if I execute the orders of the spiritual master, then I will be able to enjoy the blissful life. So any kind of thinking which is opposing service or which is opposing preaching, that is Maya. You may have a great reason why we are not preaching or why I am not doing some service or why I am not being very enthusiastic, why I am not trying to cooperate or enhance the services or push on this movement, we may have a lot of reasons and calculations, apparently sometimes based on Shastra also, but then anything that is stopping you from executing the order of the spiritual master, that is actually not Shastra, that is Maya. So that's why one has to be very vigilant, see how much enthusiastic he is to serve, and Prabhupada says in one place if somebody chants nicely his rounds and hears, then automatically he becomes very very enthusiastic. So let us not lose this opportunity, enthusiastic the way a business man is very enthusiastic to accumulate more and more money, more and more money, similarly devotee he is very enthusiastic to accumulate more and more services in his account, more service more service, more chanting more rounds, more reading, any opportunity he takes very gladly, he thanks those people who gave that opportunities, he becomes grateful to Guru, he becomes very happy and then smilingly he does his services. So this is these are some of the points that we can understand from this purport. Any questions? Yeah executing our service whatever stops it's Maya. Sometimes it may be based on the Shastra also.

## Rectifying the polluted intelligence I Sri Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa I SB 6.5.15 I 16.02.2023
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

### material nature, false ego, artificial intelligence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IS2DgfCB_Sw?t=135
**Verse References:** BG 3.27
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The aim of this revolution is to make everybody on the planet artificially intelligent."
**Summary:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa explains how material consciousness leads to unhappiness and how the bewildered soul identifies as the doer, while scientists try to master material nature without understanding the Supreme Personality. He then introduces the topic of the AI revolution and its aim to make everyone artificially intelligent.

Intelligence and material consciousness will never be happy. One must judiciously understand the activities of material nature. As stated in Bhagavad Gita, Prakrute Kriyamanani, Gunai Karmani Sarvasaha, Ahankara Vimudha Atma Kartaha Mitimanyate, the bewildered spirit soul under the influence of the three modes of material nature thinks himself to be the doer of activities which are in actuality carried out by nature. Although one follows the dictations of material nature, he happily thinks himself the master or husband of material nature. Scientists, for example, try to be the masters of material nature life after life, not caring to understand the Supreme Personality under whose direction everything will go. The aim of this revolution is to make everybody on the planet artificially intelligent. They may or may not have that actual intelligence, but the revolution is aiming to make everybody artificially intelligent.

---
### technology, artificial intelligence, revolution
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IS2DgfCB_Sw?t=302
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "You ask anything, you get the perfect, highest intelligence in that field."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the recent surge in artificial intelligence, particularly the attention garnered by ChatGPT, and how it provides intelligent solutions in various fields. He then contrasts this with the history of technology, recalling the impact of the telegram and its association with bad news.

When chart GPT was kind of announced by a marketing gimmick, it caught the attention of everybody. And then, obviously, within no time, there are millions of subscribers. And the results are very surprising. You ask anything, you get the perfect, highest intelligence in that field. They wanted a good resume for a new job and here it goes. He has a perfect resume. He wanted some help in some area. Again, you know, the perfect, intelligent solution has come to him. In fact, somebody has asked, typed, can you give some options for temple designs? Bang! Here it goes. You have a lot of designs. So, looks like the revolution that we are going to experience is going to be tremendous. Somebody traced the history of science and technology. They said they started their chart many millions of years ago. Five thousand B.C. They said it's all Stone Age. And then, around a thousand years or something, they found some gunpowder. And then, I think in 1800 or something, they found a telephone. They came up with telephone, the technology by which we can make calls. And then, in 2000, after crossing 2000, then we came up with mobile. You know, we are all witness to some of these revolutions. Our generation used to have something called a telegram and telegraph, not the telegram what we see on the mobile. And then, if any house gets a telegram those days, let us say, you know, telegram, and the postman comes with a telegram, that's all. My God, somebody passed away. Only in such cases they used to use these telegrams.

---
### technology, dependence, freedom
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IS2DgfCB_Sw?t=507
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "But then after the mobile came, one thing that has happened to our lives is we lost our independence."
**Summary:** The speaker shares personal anecdotes about the evolution of communication technology, from booking trunk calls to using STD booths, highlighting the inconveniences of the past. He contrasts this with the freedom offered by mobile phones but notes that this freedom has come at the cost of independence.

Okay, so then we all know about this mobile revolution. I don't know how many of you know, of course, in my beginning days when I joined this temple, we were in that Kalyani area, Krishna Balram temple. And if you want to make a call to your hometown, there is something called, you have to book a trunk call. You have to call the telephone exchange and then say, please book a trunk call to connect me to some, whatever that town is. And then we used to sit next to the phone and then chant our rounds, because it can take one hour or it can take four hours. So we used to sit and chant our rounds and suddenly a call comes from the telephone exchange. Yes, your call is connected. Talk, talk. And then immediately we used to talk and then first half minute will be, you know, trying to understand the sound. You know, that's a kind of technique. Not very visible, I mean, not very audible actually. And then we crossed that to reach to telephone, but that local calls are anyway available. And then STD, STD booths were there. Again, we used to stand in the queue and there used to be some STD booths nearby. And now they're all gone. Now big, big apartments have come. And then STD, when we lift the, you know, this, and then go on talking, you know, there is a display like this. And it shows how much is the amount. You know, and then if you talk for one minute, it's like, it goes into three digits, 100 rupees. You will say, how are you? One, ah, okay. Why? Because there is a tension here and tension, and they know the tension. Not much problem. So then came the mobile. Previously when mobile was not there, so let us say the telephone is ringing in the next room. We used to run. Suddenly it stops. Before you lift it, it is. And then again we'll wait for some time and then we'll go somewhere. And then again it rings. And then again you have to run. So, you know, sometimes running, running, some, you know, some people fall off and all those issues were there. And then mobile, what a freedom actually. You know, you just call and that's all. The man is available. The woman or whoever, available. He said, wow, this is very nice. All our problems are solved. You know, everything is done. But then after the mobile came, one thing that has happened to our lives is we lost our independence.

### dependence, association, intelligence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IS2DgfCB_Sw?t=688
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Prostitute of intelligence. Very intelligent invention. What is that? Mobile."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the loss of independence due to mobile phones, comparing it to a loss of freedom. He introduces the concept of the "prostitute of intelligence" to describe mobile phones and shares a story about an eye doctor's warning against using mobile phones in the dark due to potential eye damage.

Previously when call comes, okay, you called, I don't know. I am somewhere else. Lot of independence used to be there. We happily sit wherever we want. Then we know call comes, okay. Later on also we can catch up. But then after mobile came, we lost that. Brahma, Sita, Aishwarya. We have that Aishwarya being very independent and we can go on freely. Now when the call will come, we don't know. And suddenly you have to stop everything and then you have to answer that call. It has become like nature call. You have to attend to it. Unfortunately, people have made it part of their nature. Brahma, Sita, Aishwarya. That Sangha. There is so much association with that. Association with whom? It is mentioned here. Prostitute of intelligence. Very intelligent invention. What is that? Mobile. But then because of the association with that intelligence, what is happening? Brahma, Sita, Aishwarya. We have lost our independence. So that is in 2000 and all that we have experienced. Recently in Hyderabad, one eye doctor has posted a tweet, shook up some people. He said that we have diagnosed one woman with a very special eye disorder. And the reason for that is that she spends a lot of time on mobile, particularly in the night, in the dark. There are no lights and this and that and she used to read those mobile for hours together in the dark. And slowly sleep got affected. And then she started seeing flashes in our eyesight. There are floaters, dark lines. So many things he wrote. Very scary. So his advice is don't use the mobile phone in the dark. Why? Because this is the situation. Can we use it in the daytime? That nobody is talking what are the defects? What are the problems? There are a lot of problems with mobile. When you speak like this, the whole brain becomes heated up. The whole thing becomes heated up. Anyway, so Tatsanga Brahmshita Aishwaryam.

### artificial intelligence, polluted intelligence, fruitive activities

Now we are in the cusp of a new revolution. What are we going to go through? We don't know. And what is that? They are very determined. They want to make everybody artificially intelligent. One IIT, not IIT, one HOD of a computer science department he was mentioning about this chart GPT. He is saying guys wake up. You don't know what it is. And then he is telling his experience. I have 10 PhD students and they are working for the last 5-6 years to code a particular thing. And they are struggling. What they could not achieve in the last 5-6 years, I went and asked chart GPT. It has given in 10 seconds. He is saying what is the use of my PhD students? So he is saying wake up guys. You really don't know. It is only a tip of the iceberg. What chart GPT they have revealed to us is only a small tip. What exactly is the depth of you know this chart GPT or the AI revolution we really don't know. People are still assessing what will happen. Whether it will be this way, that way. What are the situations. He doesn't know. But Narada Muni knows. Ayeshwas knows. And they are saying samsarantam kubharyavat. If you take shelter of this polluted intelligence, what is polluted intelligence? Intelligence by which they want to enjoy this world. This is the polluted intelligence. And if somebody is living with this, marries this polluted intelligence, bharya kubharyavat, it is exactly like living with a prostitute. Then tadghati abudhasyeha. Such a foolish man he doesn't know where he is heading. So kim asat karma bhiva. Now this last line is a you see common in the last few verses. Every verse ends with this line. What is the use of doing these fruitive activities.

### mind, expansion, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IS2DgfCB_Sw?t=1033
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mind has the capacity to expand, it expands into hundreds and thousands of ideas."
**Summary:** The speaker references Prabhupada's explanation of the mind's capacity to expand into numerous ideas. He uses the analogy of seeing a lotus flower to illustrate how a mind attached to material enjoyment will generate countless ideas for sensual gratification, while a Krishna conscious mind will direct the thought towards Krishna.

So Prabhupada is he has quoted very important shlokas vavasaya atmika buddhi. So if you analyze how this mind is working, one of the characteristics of the mind explained by Prabhupada in Bhagavatam is that mind has the capacity to expand, it expands into hundreds and thousands of ideas, that is called expansion. So you may be with one idea, but then it expands into hundreds and thousands of ideas, one leads to two, two leads to four or eight or ten or whatever, one task unlimitedly it can expand, bahushaka. One shaka means ok there is something we can analyze and get hold of it, but it is bahushaka, it expands into many many innumerable things. So for example somebody sees this example many times previously used to discuss, let us say you see a lotus flower, who sees somebody who is kubharyavat, somebody who is married to a polluted intelligence, let us say he sees a rose flower or he sees a lotus flower, then his mind how it will work is that it expands into millions and millions of ideas of how to enjoy that rose flower or how to enjoy that lotus flower. Immediately you think ok why why not I give this to my lover or why not do this way or why not do that way and then as soon as he thinks of his lover again the next step ideas expand, a particular object triggers further ideas of how to enjoy then it goes into the next loop yeah I can meet here I can do this I can do that like that his mind is continuously expanding and he is getting lot of creative ideas what they call creative is nothing but you know all these ideas and they mix up these ideas like coffee decoction you mix coffee powder and there is hot water and there comes the decoction similarly mind also has this capacity it mixes this branch and that branch and creates a new branch called AI they have first initially they have one cricket and they have two cricket so like that mind has this capacity.

### desire, suffering, mind control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IS2DgfCB_Sw?t=1235
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The problem with this expansion is that these desires burn your heart."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the problem with the mind's expansion is that it leads to desires that burn the heart. He describes how an ordinary person is troubled by innumerable desires to enjoy, leading to attachment and the desire to implement novel ideas. This expansion implicates people in material activity, exposing them to varieties of happiness and distress.

**Summary:** The speaker explains that a properly situated devotee focuses on Krishna, seeing the lotus flower and thinking of the lotus feet of the Lord. He emphasizes that while the mind naturally expands, the key is whether it expands into Krishna consciousness or into darker ideas of material enjoyment. He highlights that a pure devotee sees everything as a trigger for Krishna consciousness and emphasizes the importance of shravan keetana and reading Prabhupada's books. He references Arjuna's question in Bhagavad Gita about how to see Krishna and Krishna's response, emphasizing that through material manifestations, one can come to the platform of Krishna consciousness. He concludes by defining a true spiritual master as one who takes away anxieties, contrasting them with those who only take away money.

## Refining Words Wisely in Speech and Prayers | Sri Satya Gaurachandra Dasa | SB 6.9.46-47| 30.01.2025
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

### prayer, knowledge, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wpbpRRajObg?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If one continues to offer such personal prayers to the Supreme Personality of Godhead, he is eligible to become a pure devotee and return home back to Godhead."
**Summary:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa reads from Srimad Bhagavatam, canto 6, chapter 9, texts 46 and 47, emphasizing the importance of personal prayers to the Supreme Personality of Godhead for purification and returning to Godhead.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevayam. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 6, Chapter 9, Texts 46 and 47. 46, I will read it by myself. śrīśuka vāca ataivam āhīdito rājan sādharam tṛdaśair harihī svam upastānam ākarṇyā prāhātān abhinanditā. Translation, Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued, O King Parīkṣit, when the demigods offered the Lord their sincere prayers in this way, the Lord listened. By his causeless mercy, being pleased, he then replied to the demigods, śrī bhagavān uvāca prīto'haṁ vahasuraśreṣṭhā madhupasthāna vidhyaya ātmaiśvaryas matipuṁsāṁ bhaktiś caivayayāmayī. Translation, and purport by His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Śrī Prabhupāda. Translation, the Supreme Personality of Godhead said, O beloved demigods, you have offered your prayers to me with great knowledge, and I am certainly most pleased with you. A person is liberated by such knowledge, and thus he remembers my exalted position, which is above the conditions of material life. Such a devotee is fully purified by offering prayers in full knowledge. This is the source of devotional service to me. Translation, and purport by His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Śrī Prabhupāda. Another name of the Supreme Personality of Godhead is Uttama Śloka, which means that he is offered prayers with selected verses. Bhakti means Shravanam Keetanam Vishnu, chanting and hearing about Lord Vishnu. Impersonalists cannot be purified, for they do not know, they do not offer personal prayers to the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Even though they sometimes offer prayers, the prayers are not directed towards the Supreme Person. Impersonalists sometimes show their incomplete knowledge by addressing the Lord, as being nameless. They always offer prayers indirectly, saying, you are this, you are that. But they do not know to whom they are praying. A devotee, however, always offers personal prayers. A devotee says, Govindam Adipurusham Tamham Bhajami. I offer my respectful obeisances unto Govinda and to Krishna. That is the way to offer prayers. If one continues to offer such personal prayers to the Supreme Personality of Godhead, he is eligible to become a pure devotee and return home back to Godhead. This ends the Bhaktivedanta Prabodho.
