So we are reading from the chapter, The Appearance of the Demon, Vrutrasura. And about three ślokas before, we have seen that Vrutrasura already appears as the first son. And the demigods, being afraid of Vrutrasura, they approach Vishnu and they offer their heartfelt prayers to Vishnu. And by listening to those prayers, Lord Vishnu is now addressing the demigods and he says, Prithoham Vahasurashtreshta. He says, I am very pleased with your prayers. So this is the success of life. You are standing in front of Lord Vishnu and he is saying, I am satisfied with you. And that is our success. Although the demigods approached him for some material benefit, they are his servants administrating the universe. So they have difficulty. In difficulty, a devotee has no other shelter than Krishna. So the Lord, when he saw that the demigods are approaching him, the first thing that he did was, he carefully listened to them. Here it is mentioned, Akarnya. Akarnya means, the Lord carefully listened to the demigods and their prayers. The Lord is so powerful that he listens to what is spoken and what is not spoken. What is there in the heart, what is there in the chitta, everything he understands. And it is his causeless mercy that he listens to such prayers. Actually, Krishna listens to every jiva. He is always listening to us and he is trying to communicate to us but then,

And then he's using the word locustandy. It's very interesting, you know, everywhere you see Prabhupada is trying to get an appropriate word to really describe Krishna's glories in that particular past time or the scenario. But unfortunately, English doesn't have that kind of a support. So whatever is available, the best available, those words Prabhupada has taken and he has described.

Let us say an LLP preacher is going to meet a donor and let us say he is a very big donor, highly respected in the society. LLP preacher is in anxiety. His anxiety is, now what should I say? So then some ISKCON follower, not the Ashram devotee, somebody went to him. And then, while discussing, the doctor was talking about somebody passing away in his family. And this is his family. Iskand whatever follower, he said a Dheera is not affected by such changes of bodies. The doctor was very upset. One word only used, he was very upset. And then the doctor said from now onwards, I will not see any Iskand follower. And then just nearby standing was Sarvabhaum Bhattacharya, the leading Mayavadi of the country. We are talking about a leading person. He trains up hundreds of Mayavadis and he sees Lord Chaitanya in a beautiful form. Immediately he takes him to his house and he puts a little cotton swab just to see whether he is there or not. So he understands that he is alive. Then he realizes that he is experiencing highest emotions. There are some technical words mentioned, not able to recollect. Very interesting to read those purpose. Then Sarvabhaum Bhattacharya says, you are a young sanyasi and you are also looking very beautiful and handsome. How will you be able to manage your sanyas without understanding Vedanta? How will you be able to manage your sanyas without understanding Vedanta? How will you be able to manage your sanyas without understanding Vedanta? Vedanta means pumping in renunciation. Not this, not this body, not this world. No, no, no, no, neti, neti, neti. So Sarvabhaum Bhattacharya is feeling that I have to pump in a lot of renunciation. Then only Lord Chaitanya will be able to maintain his sanyas as a young person. So he tells Lord Chaitanya that I want to tell you the whole Vedanta. So he tells Lord Chaitanya that I want to tell you the whole Vedanta. So he tells Lord Chaitanya that I want to tell you the whole Vedanta. I said yes. And you see how Lord Chaitanya, his behavior, he simply sits and only seven days he goes on talking about Vedanta, Vedanta Sutra and all those things. And Lord Chaitanya is simply hearing. Sarvabhaum Bhattacharya is not able to understand whether Lord Chaitanya understood or not. He is not. So he openly says, you know not even one sound came from you, are you understanding or what is happening? Do you understand Vedanta? So Lord Chaitanya says, I understand Vedanta but I have not understood your way of telling Vedanta. Then Sarvabhauma Bhattacharya says, because after seven days he spoke and then he says, let me hear what you want to say. What is your version of Vedanta? ## SB 3.26.15 | HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa | Hare Krishna Golden Temple
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

### Saguna Brahman, Nirguna Brahman, Time
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3GWRLrkxmYM?t=173
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When Brahman is mixed with the three qualities, goodness, passion and ignorance, there results the material expansion, which is sometimes called Saguna Brahman, and which consists of three, these 25 elements."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the difference between Saguna and Nirguna Brahman, and how the material expansion results from Brahman mixing with the three qualities, consisting of 25 elements including time.

एतावान, so much, एवा, just, संख्यातह, enumerated, ब्रह्मनह, अफ ब्रह्मन, सगुनस्या, with material qualities, हा, indeed, सन्निवेशह, arrangement, मया, by me, प्रोक्तह, spoken, यहा, which, कालह, time, पंचविम्शकह, the twenty-fifth, translation, purported by his divine grace, एसी बक्त्वदान्त स्वामिश्व प्रवपात, all these are considered the qualified Brahman, the mixing element which is known as time, is counted as the twenty-fifth element. All these are considered the qualified Brahman, the mixing element which is known as time, is counted as the twenty-fifth element, purported by एसी बक्त्वदान्त स्वामिश्व प्रवपात, according to the Vedic version, there is no existence beyond Brahman, सर्वं खलू यदं ब्रह्मान्, प्रपोर्ट बायेश प्रवपात, according to the Vedic version, there is no existence beyond Brahman, सर्वं खलू यदं ब्रह्मान्, प्रपोर्ट बायेश प्रणिग, all the earth and the matter are same as Brahma. When Brahman is mixed with the three qualities, goodness, passion and ignorance, there results the material expansion, which is sometimes called Saguna Brahman, and which consists of three, these 25 elements. In the Nirguna Brahman, where there is no material contamination, or in the spiritual world, the three modes, goodness, passion and ignorance, are not present. Where Nirguna Brahman is found, simple, unalloyed goodness prevails. Saguna Brahman is described by the Sankhya system of philosophy as consisting of 25 elements, including the time factor, past, present and future.

So in the last few verses we have seen Kapila Muni has enumerated 24 elements which are found in this cosmic manifestation; they are the basic bricks by which the whole manifestation is built, and here he is introducing the 25th element, is the 25th element which is actually the mixing element in such a way that the time is the one which brings in this manifestation, and this time is also considered one of this elements, that means 25th element, and he also says.

### Brahman, Krishna, Mayavadi Philosophy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3GWRLrkxmYM?t=542
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everything is Brahman and it is connected to Parabrahman. Saguna refers to the living entity who is covered by these 25 elements. Living entity is Brahman by nature, he is a spirit in nature, but that spirit is covered by these 25 elements."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that everything is Brahman and connected to Parabrahman, with Saguna referring to the living entity covered by the 25 elements. He contrasts this with Mayavadi philosophy, which considers Krishna as merely a human being or Saguna Brahman.

One of the shaktis of the supreme personality of Godhead, so Krishna is spiritual, he is Parabrahman, and the energy that comes from him is also Brahman. So the understanding that it is also connected to Krishna, it is sacred, and we have to deal with this cosmic manifestation in order to please the Supreme Lord. This is the understanding; one should not think that this is just matter meant for my enjoyment and let me exploit the way I want, that is a wrong understanding. So here it is mentioned, everything is Brahman and it is connected to Parabrahman. So Saguna refers to the living entity who is covered by these 25 elements. Living entity is Brahman by nature, he is a spirit in nature, but that spirit is covered by these 25 elements. These 25 elements are covering the spirit, the Brahman, and the spirit is covered by and that Brahman is known as Saguna Brahman. There is one more Brahman that is called Nirguna Brahman who is none other than the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Krishna is Nirguna Brahman, the conditioned living entities are the Saguna Brahman. Everything is Brahman and everybody is one is a wrong philosophy to think that there is only one all pervading impersonal Brahman and there are no forms beyond the forms that we see in this world. These are the concepts of a Mayavadi philosophy. They consider when Krishna comes to this world, they consider him as a person who is not a human being, they consider him as a human being, Saguna Brahman.

### Time, Material Manifestation, Analogy

**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of building a tower to illustrate how time is the mixing element in material manifestation, requiring ingredients to be mixed over a period to achieve a desired shape.

### Time, Change, Perception
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3GWRLrkxmYM?t=1108
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You can see the effect, but you cannot see the process how Kala has deteriorated or changed that person; that's why the time, how it acts, nobody can figure out what process it uses, how it is really acting, nobody can see."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the example of observing a person's physical changes over time to illustrate how time acts imperceptibly, with its process unseen but its effects clearly visible.

Imperceptibly, for example you see a person today, he is looking, you know, something, and after say one year you see the same person, you can see that he has either become thin or he has become fat or he has become little older, you can see the effect, but you cannot see the process how Kala has deteriorated or changed that person, that body. The bodies are continuously, there is a lot of creation and destruction going on, so many cells are getting produced, so many cells are getting destroyed every second. This is a scientific fact that means every second there is a creation, there is a destruction, there is a maintenance going on in this body at the cell level, and when the destruction of the cells is more than the creation, gradually the decay will be visible. You really don't know what process is going on inside, you can only see the effect; that's why the time, how it acts, nobody can figure out what process it uses, how it is really acting, nobody can see, nobody can see the process, but they can see the final effect.

### Time, Decay, Impersonal Force
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3GWRLrkxmYM?t=1207
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is how the time functions, imperceptible, how it is acting nobody can perceive, but it is acting silently. It is an impersonal, imperceptible, and powerful force which is acting in this world."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the example of a building deteriorating over time to illustrate how time functions imperceptibly, acting as a silent, impersonal, and powerful force in the world.

And what is that effect? That person's body has changed. For example, any building you take, you have built a building, and after one year you will see the paint has gone, is peeled off, and here and there some bricks are moving, and some things are not working, some way the colors are changing, all these things you will observe, but you don't know the process by which it happens. This is how the time functions, imperceptible, how it is acting nobody can perceive, but it is acting silently. We may not be able to see, we may not be able to understand how it is going on, but silently the time is working, and at some point of time you can only see the impact or the effect of this process, what the process that the time is going implementing. So it is an impersonal, imperceptible, and powerful force which is acting in this world.

**Key Quote:** "If we want to move from saguna life, that means the life full of limitations, miseries, and challenges, to a life of freedom, nirguna, freedom happiness and enjoyment, the only way is we have to utilize time very carefully."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of utilizing time carefully to transition from a life of limitations (saguna) to a life of freedom and happiness (nirguna), highlighting that everyone receives an equal amount of time.

And time slips away without noticing anything. If we want to move from saguna life, that means the life full of limitations, miseries, and challenges, to a life of freedom, nirguna, freedom happiness and enjoyment, the only way is we have to utilize time very carefully. One has to utilize this precious resource in one's life, and this time is so nice, everybody has equal time every day. I get the same number of hours the way a big man gets, the prime minister has the same time, I also have the same time, you know, an ordinary person also has the same time, see equality. God is equally kind to everybody, he gave the same time to everybody, but how one utilizes this time in order to grow in his life, that makes the difference.

### Time, Change, Destruction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3GWRLrkxmYM?t=1624
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That's the effect you can see, but what actually going on nobody can see; this is how time works, a silent killer. Kala's main function is to destroy; everything becomes chaotic over time, everything becomes decayed over time, everything gets disorganized over time; this is how the time is."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the example of seeing the changes in पल्लकी carriers over the years to illustrate the silent, destructive nature of time, emphasizing that time's main function is to destroy and bring chaos.

I used to see these पल्लकी carriers carrying the पल्लकी year after year, so when I went recently and saw them, one striking thing was all their hairs have become completely white, not even one single hair is looking little blackish, everything is turned, that's the effect you can see, but what actually going on nobody can see; this is how time works, a silent killer. When Arjuna asks Krishna who are you when Krishna is exhibiting the universal form, Krishna says I am the time and I am the destroyer of the three worlds. Kala's main function is to destroy; everything becomes chaotic over time, everything becomes decayed over time, everything gets disorganized over time; this is how the time is.

Unless we put endeavor and we put to bring in some control, some system, the rule is it decays, degrades. That's why when he is teaching Bhagavad Gita to Arjuna, Krishna explains that it is yoga nastaha, whatever the science I have taught you, whatever the science of connecting to Krishna, connecting to the Supreme Lord, that yoga, yoga means connection with Krishna, that is lost, how it is lost over the time. If you just leave anything like that, it is only decaying decaying, it is lost, it will be lost. Actually Chandrika Pandit is not saying not even one minute, he is saying one moment, but let us say you take one minute, it became 843, 842 cannot be restored even by paying one million dollars or one million gold coins, you cannot restore. So Chandrika Pandit says imagine such costly resource it is because even one million dollars cannot bring it back, such a costly resource and imagine you waste it for some trivial things, you waste it for unnecessary things, you misuse it, let us say how much? ### Time, Krishna Consciousness, Ekadashi
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3GWRLrkxmYM?t=2239
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every minute, every second, let us say if I can use it for Krishna, that is my life; this is the thinking of a Madhyamadhikari, he doesn't tolerate wasting one second or one moment without Krishna consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of utilizing every moment for Krishna consciousness, highlighting the intolerance of a Madhyamadhikari towards wasting time, and redefines Ekadashi as saving and dedicating time to Krishna.

Every minute, every second, let us say if I can use it for Krishna, that is my life; this is the thinking of a Madhyamadhikari, he doesn't tolerate wasting one second or one moment without Krishna consciousness. So devotees, they should become very very expert in utilizing this time, they should not waste time. Let us say we go to, you know, for bodily works or necessities, don't spend too much time, quickly finish and ensure that we give maximum time for Krishna's service, reading Prabhupada books, chanting more rounds, that's when Ekadeshi deity said that your bodily needs you minimize because when we go for eating, minimum half an hour goes, or if you know you are chit chatting with somebody, 45 minutes goes, imperceptible, you don't even know, after coming out you will realize, oh my god, so much time is gone, and then once you eat, then you have to go to washroom, this, that and all, again time goes, so an Ekadeshi deity is advised that you minimize all this, don't eat and don't do any of the simply focus on Krishna's service, chant, read, do service more and more, this is Ekadeshi means really, this is what it is, fasting is not Ekadeshi, okay, that is one element, but actually use the time, save time for Krishna and use the time for Krishna's service, this is this is Sampoorna Ekadeshi, save time and give time to Krishna, this is what is required.

## Sense Enjoyment Is Regarded as Poison | Sri Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa | SB 3.23.53 | 17.10.2024
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

### sense gratification, time, spiritual advancement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0WcBYB5zBmA?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We have wasted so much time in sense gratification."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on Devahuti's lamentation about wasting time in sense gratification and her desire for spiritual advancement.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Madbhavatam, Kanto 3, Chapter 23, Verse 5, Devahuti's Lamentation. Etavatalam Kalena, Vyatikrantena Me Prabho, Indriyartha Prasangenam, Pratityakta Paratmanaham. Translation: Until now we have simply wasted so much of our time. Insightful. Sense gratification, neglecting to cultivate knowledge of the Supreme Lord. Purport by Srila Prabhupada: Human life is not meant to be wasted like that of the animals in sense gratificatory activities. Animals always engage in eating, sleeping, fearing and mating, but that is not the engagement of the human being. Although because of the material body, there is need of sense gratification according to a regulative principle. So in effect, Devahuti said to her husband, so far we have these daughters and we have enjoyed material life in the aerial mansion, traveling all over the universe. These boons have come by your grace, but they have all been for sense gratification. Now there must be something for my spiritual advancement. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Parabharat. So Devahuti is speaking to her exalted husband, Kardama. And what is the reason? So Devahuti is speaking to her exalted husband, Kardama, and here she is summarizing her experience and she is saying we have wasted so much time in sense gratification.

---
### sense gratification, dissatisfaction, material arrangements
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0WcBYB5zBmA?t=112
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Despite so much enjoyment and arrangements, she is not feeling satisfied; there is something missing."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Devahuti's realization that despite having immense material facilities and sense gratification, she feels a void in her heart, highlighting the inherent dissatisfaction in material pursuits.

We have wasted so much time and is there any use? Alam means for nothing. For nothing we have wasted so much time in indriyartha prasangena, simply in sense gratification. So whatever grand arrangements were made, so much hangama was created for sense gratification. We have seen in the many shlokas, these arrangements for sense gratification are incomparable. In the entire history, the human history, you will not find one instance where someone is able to make such arrangements for sense gratification. There is an aerial mansion, that means a flying town, a township or a big building, palatial building, and then thousands of young maidservants, all kinds of food, all kinds of birds and animals, and all pleasurable things, whatever a human being desires in life, it is there in hundreds and thousands of multiplication. And then they have spent so much time in sense gratification and finally Devahuti is saying we simply wasted our time. Etavata alam kalena vidhikrantena me prabhu indriyartha prasangana because the sense gratification is by nature is like that, doesn't satisfy the heart. One may be having all kinds of arrangements for sensual pleasures, summarized into this eating, sleeping, fearing and mating, but whatever may be those arrangements, and he may really have all such, you know, everything in place and he may really have all such, but still there is some vacuum in the heart already. Devahuti is feeling that vacuum, despite so much enjoyment and arrangements, she is not feeling satisfied; there is something missing. Actually Devahuti, you can see very advanced, yeah, this is not ordinary consciousness.

### happiness, rat race, materialism

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker describes the universal pursuit of happiness and how it manifests as a 'rat race' in the material world, where people chase objects and achievements, often symbolized by money, seeking satisfaction.

In this world also, there is a race going on for happiness, because every human being, in fact every living entity, wants to be happy like that. It's an instinct from the soul. Soul wants to be continuously happy. And so, although transcendental and spiritual in constitution, when the living entity comes to this world, its search for happiness turns into acquiring more and more objects for enjoyment. Its urge for happiness turns into simply chasing one facility after the other, or one object after the other. This is how everybody is in a race. They also know, they say rat race. They also say this. There is a rat race. What is this rat race? Everybody, you know, you see that 100 meters Olympics, there is one race, running race, right? Everybody, they have a beginning point and then they all race. One wants to go faster than the other, so that he can reach the destination. Then he'll get a medal. You see how the whole race has been built into our consciousness. So then, in the day-to-day life also, it's the same thing. Similar race. There are some few objects available, because nothing is unlimited here. There are some limited things are there. And then everybody thinks, if I can get that object, then I'll be happy. This is only thinking. That's all. In the mind. So, with that kind of an intelligence, that kind of a mentality, everybody tries to chase those objects. And finally, if somebody happens to achieve it, then immediately there will be a flash in the media, and all the channels, so and so has reached this position, and then everybody glorifies. ### money, Kali Yuga, dissatisfaction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0WcBYB5zBmA?t=471
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "In this Kali Yuga particularly, it doesn't matter what kind of a goonda he is. You know, if there is a lot of money with him, immediately all good qualities are there."
**Summary:** The speaker critiques the glorification of wealth in Kali Yuga, even when acquired through questionable means, and contrasts it with the superior facilities enjoyed by Kardama Muni, ultimately reiterating Devahuti's realization of wasted time despite material abundance.

One of the things that everybody chases is money. And then if somebody is able to beat everybody else, by whatever means, legal, illegal, whatever, he beats everybody else, and then acquires a lot of money, he will be glorified. In this Kali Yuga particularly, it doesn't matter what kind of a goonda he is. You know, if there is a lot of money with him, immediately all good qualities are there. He is a sadhu, he is everything. This is the scenario. And after reaching that position, billionaire, millionaire, whatever that title may be, that position, one thinks, I can be very happy. But as of today, the technological advancement is such that, you cannot create a flying palace, an aerial mansion, as of today. You may be a big billionaire, but then you will not be able to create such palace. Now imagine Kardama Muni, he has created that palace. So, in terms of the facilities, he has bettered the facilities created by the so-called billionaires of this age. Much better. So, after experiencing all such facilities for sense gratification, Devahuti is saying, Paritaktva Paratmana, Paritaktva Paratmana, I have neglected, we have neglected to cultivate the knowledge of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. This is her realization. Paritaktva Paratmana, Paritaktva Paratmana, and she is saying, we simply wasted our time.

### fear, happiness, spiritual side of life

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that happiness is unattainable without addressing the spiritual aspect of life, as material achievements always come with inherent fears and anxieties.

Continuously everybody is anxious and fearful. Then where is happiness? We are thinking my happiness is achieving that position. I have some problem. If I can resolve this problem, that is I am happy. But even if you achieve some greatest position, it always comes with a fear. Even the greatest position comes to you with a baggage, with a fear attached to it. Every possession is like that. So this is this world. So here Devahuti, she got all kinds of best facilities, a sense of gratification. And she is saying, we simply wasted our time. So, why Paritaktva Paratma? Because we neglected to cultivate knowledge of the Supreme Lord. Actually, this is the right conclusion. Happiness is impossible if one doesn't look at the spiritual side of life. If one neglects the spiritual side and thinks that by material progress and acquiring so many objects or reaching some material position, I will be happy. That is illusory thinking.

Because he is neglecting one important aspect of his life. What is that? That yes, I am a spirit soul and body is only a little portion of my existence here. Body is also real and soul is real. Of course, body is real means it is temporarily existing. But my idea of happiness is if I can satisfy the body, I am happy. No, that's not true. Because you means not only body, but there is the spirit soul. So, if somebody thinks I have to satisfy my body, I have to satisfy my soul also, then at least thinking wise is right. At least he is considering the whole picture. Otherwise, with a partial picture in mind, with a little understanding in his mind, if he molds his life simply to enhance his enjoyment of the body, then he will be deceived. Ultimately, he will remain dissatisfied. That's what is happening to Devahati.

### Krishna consciousness, devotion, sense gratification

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of focusing intelligence on developing Krishna consciousness and avoiding distractions that dilute devotion and increase the desire for sense gratification.

So for a devotee who has come to Krishna consciousness, he has to, his intelligence has to be focused on developing his Krishna consciousness. Intelligence cannot be diluted and diverted to many other objects. The only object that he has to focus is on Krishna. For a devotee, Krishna is the only target and object. As soon as one focuses on any other object, his intelligence becomes many branched. It becomes diverted, diluted. Intensity won't be there on Krishna. The moment we develop attachment for anything other than Krishna, then our intensity on the Supreme Lord is diluted. This is very simple to understand. The moment we divert our attention from Krishna, automatically the desire for sense gratification grows. Krishna Surya Sama. So Krishna means Krishna's service. And then Maya Andhakaar and Maya means sense gratification. These are the two sides. On one side there is sense gratification, and the other side is service. If somebody is absorbed in service, then there is no sense gratification for personal thing. And if somebody is absorbed in personal sense gratification, then there is no peace, there is no satisfaction.

### sense gratification, poison, consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0WcBYB5zBmA?t=1170
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Acharyas call sense gratification as poison."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the conditioned soul's perception of sense gratification as nectar with the Acharyas' view of it as poison, emphasizing that the poison lies in the consciousness of being an independent enjoyer.

So that's why devotees who are absorbed in transcendental service to Krishna, is automatically they are relieved from the effects of this sense gratification. Actually, a conditioned soul thinks sense gratification is like a nectar, a very, very pleasing nectar. But Acharyas call sense gratification as poison. As soon as we somebody engages in sense gratification, that's considered poison. He is taking poison. It's a completely a different understanding. Let us say somebody eats some nice sweet or whatever on the roadside he is going and Acharyas are saying this is poison. He is taking poison. He is saying no, no, Prabhu, this is a very good brand. They are there for last 100 years. They do all nice. There is no poison there. But why is it that you are saying it's a poison? The poison is not at the physical level. The poison is at the level of the consciousness. The consciousness is poisoned. So, by thinking that yes, I am the enjoyer of all these varieties of food or varieties of facilities, this very thinking, this is the poison. To think that I am an enjoyer independent of Krishna, that is the poison.

Then I am the possessor, I am the owner, not you. The moment you think you are the owner, you will get fear. You put Krishna as the only friend, then your social account is perfect. My only friend is Krishna. No other friends are allowed here. Your Facebook or whatever that you create. So, these things may look theoretical, but actually these are the most practical things. Otherwise, somebody simply lives an illusory life and wastes his time. What greatest example you need, just look at this example of Kardama and Devahuti. We have heard all colorful things and then at the end, Devahuti is saying that simply we have wasted our time. Actually, this should be the consciousness of a devotee. The moment our mind gets diverted towards sense gratification, we should feel that simply I am wasting time. And in this age, you imagine such time-wasting things are so easily available. This is what you have to do. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says, he explains in one of the shlokas, He says, nishkinchan asya bhagavat bhajanonmukhasya paramparam jigamisho bhavasagar asya sandarshanam visayanam. So, Mahaprabhu is lamenting, He is saying, Alas, for a person who is seriously desiring to cross the ocean, material ocean, and engage in transcendental loving service of the Lord, or without material motives, seeing a materialist engaged in Sanskritification, or seeing a woman who is similarly interested, is more abominable than drinking poison willingly.

### reality, Guru and Krishna, service

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The reality is that, the Guru and Krishna, they are the ones who are doing, but they are so kind, they give credit to their devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing the reality that Guru and Krishna are the doers, and devotees should humbly recognize their role as instruments in service, avoiding personal ambition and cultivating a continuous relationship with the divine.

We have come here to learn, to see the reality. The reality is that, the Guru and Krishna, they are the ones who are doing, but they are so kind, they give credit to their devotees. Although Krishna does everything, Guru does everything, they give the credit. So when that credit comes, a devotee thinks inside, actually I don't deserve this, I simply tried doing something on the order of my Guru, and by their mercy, something has come. And so he, factually understands that, you know, I didn't do anything, some little endeavor, and that too by Guru's blessing, and I got everything. So devotee, by giving such credit to Guru and Krishna, he enjoys an ongoing relation. He sees Guru and Krishna in day-to-day life, and then he enjoys an ongoing relationship. He doesn't want to take anything from Guru and Krishna. He is always thinking how to give. Even if they give some big name and fame, he is thinking how I should give more and more to Guru and Krishna. He is not deviated, diverted. So that's why his personal ambitions are very dangerous. They are vishabhakshana. And this time, he is thinking, how can I do this? How can I do this? And these statements of Bhagavatam are not palatable at all. How sense gratification is compared to visha? So this is not palatable, but this is the reality. This is the truth. So devotee, who is sincerely seeking the truth of life, is really want to situate himself in absolute truth, then this is, this is the real nature of this world. Sense gratification is simply poison, and then a devotee is careful not to fall for it. Now how he can be careful? Simply by engaging in Krishna's service. Either by chanting, or by rendering physical services, or intellectual services, or whatever. As soon as one is absorbed, in service to Krishna, for Krishna's satisfaction, automatically is freed from sense gratification. Otherwise, the senses require engagement, they pull the person towards more and more sense gratification. So one has to be careful to be in the service of the Lord. Any questions?

## Significance of Cultivating Emotional Intelligence | Sri Satya Gaur Chandra Dasa | SB 3.22.24-25
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

### Vedic culture, family responsibility, separation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/95RoFf_e9tg?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "A father always remains in anxiety until he can hand over his grown-up daughter to a suitable boy."
**Summary:** The speaker reads verses from Srimad-Bhagavatam describing Swayambhuva Manu's feelings of relief and separation as he gives his daughter in marriage, highlighting the Vedic emphasis on responsibility and the woman's dependence on male figures throughout her life.

OM NAMO BHAGAVATE VASUDEVAYA. Reading from Srimad-Bhagavatam, Canto 3, Chapter 22, entitled, The Marriage of Kardama Muni and Devahuti. Verse number 24, 25. Verse 24, I will read myself. PRATAM DUHITARAM SAMRAT SADRIT SAYADRATVYATAH UPADRUHYACHA BAHUPYAM AVTARANTHYON MATHITASHAYAH. Translation: Thus relieved of his responsibility by handing over his daughter to a suitable man, Swayambhuva Manu, his mind agitated by feeling of separation, embraced his affectionate daughter with both his arms. Purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada, Jai! A father always remains in anxiety until he can hand over his grown-up daughter to a suitable boy. A father and mother's responsibility for children continue until they marry them to suitable spouses. When the father is able to perform that duty, he is relieved of his responsibility. Verse number 25, ASATNUVAM STADVIRAHAM MUNCHAN BASPATALAMMUHO ASINCHAD AMBAVATSETI NETRO DAIR DUHITU SITHAH. ASATNUVAM STADVIRAHAM MUNCHAN BASPATALAMMUHO ASINCHAD AMBAVATSETI NETRO DAIR DUHITU SITHAH. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada, Jai! The emperor was unable to bear the separation of his daughter. Therefore, tears poured from his eyes again and again, drenching his daughter's head as he cried, My dear mother, my dear daughter. Purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada, Jai! The word Amba is significant. A father sometimes addresses his daughter in affection as mother, and sometimes as my darling. The feeling of separation utters because until the daughter is married, she remains the daughter of the father. But after her marriage, she is no longer claimed as a daughter in the family. She must go to the husband's house, for after marriage, she becomes the property of the husband. According to Manu Samhita, a woman is never independent. She must remain the property of the father while she is not married, and she must remain the property of the husband until she is elderly and has grown up children of her own. In old age, when the husband has taken sannyas and left home, she remains the property of the sons. A woman is always dependent either upon the father, husband, or elderly sons. That will be exhibited in the life of Devahuti. Devahuti's father handed over responsibility, for her, to the husband, Kardama Muni. And in the same way, Kardama Muni also left home, giving the responsibility to his son, Kapiladev. This narration will describe these events one after another. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Purport.

---
### Vedic culture, family, affection, responsibility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/95RoFf_e9tg?t=281
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The Vedic culture is about responsibilities, and it is not about rights."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the verses, emphasizing the Vedic culture's focus on responsibilities over rights, the affection within families, and the importance of taking care of daughters, culminating in the concept of Kanyadaan, the charitable giving away of a daughter in marriage.

So as soon as the marriage got over, the Swayambhu Manus, the Swayambhu Manu's daughter, was given to a great sage by name Kardama Muni. Pratham Duhitaram Samrat, Sadrukshaya Gatavyata, immediately he is relieved from his responsibility. So the Vedic culture is about responsibilities, and it is not about rights. Fighting for rights is a modern phenomena, but Vedic culture is full of responsibilities, with affection and love. So here we can see, Swayambhu Manu, he is so affectionate towards the daughter, and as soon as she is married, he felt relieved of the responsibility. But immediately, he became very anxious, because he realized, now my daughter is going to go away, to the husband's house. So immediately he became very, feelings of separation appeared in his heart. So a father and mother, they feel responsibility, that the children should settle in life, and as soon as they marry them, they feel a certain relief of the responsibility. So here, the Swayambhu Manu, Ashaknuvam Stadviraham, the separation from the daughter was so intense, that Munchan Vashpakalamuhu, again and again, is shedding tears, and drenching the daughter's hair. Asinchad ambavatseti, my dear mother, my dear darling, my dear daughter. So he was crying, by calling her, as mother and daughter, Netrodair Duhitu Shikha. So, he hugged his daughter, and then crying, by calling these names. In our childhood also, we would have seen, sometimes parents, they call the children, Telugu, if you know, they'll say, Rananna, Emma, Amma, Bawanna, Bum, Melara, that's the same thing here, he's calling her mother, and also, my daughter. So these are the very affectionate dealings used to be there, among the family members. There used to be a wonderful bondage, within the family, husband, wife is attached to husband, and husband is taking care of the family, the children are very affectionate to the parents, and then the parents are very taking care of the children, even the smallest of the sensitivities, feelings, are exchanged, and enjoyed. The family has ample time, to exchange all this wonderful, relational feelings, among themselves, so that's why, when the Swayambhu Manu, was, you know, as soon as he realized, that he is getting separated from the daughter, immediately he became very anxious, and he started crying, with the tears, coming from his eyes, again and again. So this was the kind of a condition, the daughter is always taken care of, so nicely because, she is a greatest asset for the family, and so there is to treat her with great affection, and if she is going away, so that is called Kanyadaan. Actually, no one can estimate, you know, you cannot pay any amount for her, she can only be given away, in charity, by the father, to the husband, the boy.

---
### Krishna consciousness, family, attachment, modern society
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/95RoFf_e9tg?t=589
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The primary feeling of attachment is for Vishnu, or Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the strength of family bonds in Vedic culture stemmed from their shared attachment to Krishna, contrasting this with modern society where the focus on material necessities leaves little time for cultivating such spiritual connections, leading to unhappiness and family discord.

Because, the whole focus was, on, feeling, separation, from, the Supreme, Personality of Godhead. The primary feeling of attachment is for Vishnu, or Krishna. Why family bondage was so strong, because of their attachment, to the Supreme Lord. He is the center of attraction, and center of affection, for everybody. So, feeling attraction, to Krishna, and separation, from Him, is the highest necessity of a human being. If the human being is trained up, from childhood, in this aspect, of developing attachment, to Krishna, the boy will become a good devotee, responsible, and a girl becomes a good devotee, and she becomes chased, and the family will be flourishing. Because of there is the lack of cultural training, in this modern world, first of all, just for getting our own food, both husband and wife have to work hard, throughout the day, and there is no time, for any other aspects of life. They don't even get time, for even the basic activities. So, that is the kind of situation, today. But, we are talking about, a scenario, where, you know, the Vedic culture, was very simple. People never focused on necessities of body. They focused on this prime necessity of the soul, which is to develop attachment, to Krishna. So, within that primary attachment, to Krishna, they used to have secondarily, a lot of attachment, to this family, dealings. So, because those things are missing, nowadays, even materially, people are not happy. Forget about spiritual life. Materially also, there is so much of restlessness. They are not happy. The families, the quarrels in a family, are increasing. The differences within the family, are increasing. If a society has to be happy, then the family units have to be happy. This is the mission of Krishna consciousness.

### Krishna consciousness, family, culture, children
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/95RoFf_e9tg?t=773
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Our movement is about bringing happiness and prosperity in the family units by making Krishna as the center of the attraction for all the family members."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the Krishna consciousness movement's aim to bring happiness to families by centering them around Krishna, sharing a positive anecdote about a culture camp for children and highlighting the affection of a father as described in the scriptures.

Our movement is about bringing happiness and prosperity in the family units by making Krishna as the center of the attraction for all the family members. Recently, we concluded the culture camp. It's a summer camp for children, during their summer holidays. They all come and participate. So, in 21 days, the children learnt a lot of cultural aspects. Our Indian culture, this Vaishnava culture, they have learnt nice, you know, aspects of this. And I heard some feedback from the parents. They were so happy. They are saying, my children are now able to recite some Sanskrit slokas, which I never ever heard either myself, or in my family. They are happy to see. In one apartment, I think Pushpa apartment, one parent, you know, he generally goes for some exercise, and you know, that some maybe, they have a gym there. And then nearby, there is one room, where this culture camp was going on. And, you know, he used to hear every day, because he goes on time, and then this also happens on time. So, there is, you know, some Kirtans, and you know, some Puja, with ringing bell. And he used to hear all those things, and then he used to do his own exercise, and all that. So, as soon as this 21 days are over, he sent a big message to us, Swamiji, I am missing all this. I hope that it continues for some more time, but, you know, I am missing this. So, one feels nice, in a spiritual environment, that is natural. ## Sri Maitreya Muni Explains the Process of Creation of Material World I SB 3.5.27 I 11.01.2024
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

### matter, consciousness, soul
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2FFahOBMS8c?t=246
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Matter cannot animate without the presence of the consciousness, without the presence of the soul."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the difference between matter and consciousness, arguing that matter cannot animate without the presence of the soul, a point scientists often miss.

We can pile up ingredients and construct a house, construct a car, construct a mobile, but basic ingredients are now manifested in the form of Mahatatwam, the original matter. Matter in its primary, primordial state is called Pradhana or Mahatatwam. So ultimately, although these manifestations are there, whole matter can be traced to a primordial state called Pradhana. That's why many scientists, they propose this, that everything is coming from one single molecule, one single particle. Actually, it's a fact, whole matter ultimately becomes Pradhana, the abhyaktat, unmanifested form. But what they do not know is that matter cannot animate without the presence of the consciousness.

---
### soul, God, Mahat Tattva, consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2FFahOBMS8c?t=418
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mahat Tattva is actually a shadow of pure consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that living entities originate from God, not matter, and are injected into matter, which is still evolving. He introduces the concept of Mahat Tattva as the total consciousness and a shadow of pure consciousness, connected to the Supreme Being.

Here we can see, Vijnana Atma Dehastham Vishwam Venjamsthamonudha. The Lord has injected these seeds of consciousness, that means the souls, has injected the living entities into this matter. So the living entities are coming from God, they are not coming from matter. They have just been injected into matter. Matter is still evolving. How can living entity be matter then? Living entities are existing even before matter manifested. They are existing in the body of Mahavishnu. Only when he glanced, then the living entities came, and into the matter they are merged, they are injected. So this is, we can see in the last few classes, this is the clarity that Bhagavatam is bringing. The living entities are coming, the living entities are not matter, they cannot be reduced to a material particle. They are different energies of the Supreme Lord. So here Prabhupada reveals some interesting aspects of Mahat Tattva. He says Mahat Tattva is the total consciousness because a portion of it is represented in everyone as the intellect. So Mahat Tattva is the total consciousness. Now what is this total consciousness? Here it is mentioned, the Mahat Tattva is directly connected with the supreme consciousness of the Supreme being, but still it appears as matter. Mahat Tattva or shadow of pure consciousness is germinating place of all creation.

---
### spiritual world, material world, imitation, shadow
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2FFahOBMS8c?t=540
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This entire world is a shadow world, or an imitation world."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of children playing with toys, the speaker explains that the material world is an imitation of the spiritual world, created with concepts taken from the spiritual realm but made of different, temporary materials.

Let us try to understand this. So Prabhupada is saying Mahatatva is actually a shadow of pure consciousness. If you see the elements that we see in this world, like Bhoomi, Apaha, Vata, Anala, fire and then air, and then ether. If you see these elements, or you take the subtle elements, mind, intelligence and ego, they are also called shadow elements. They are shadow of the original elements in the spiritual world. This entire world is a shadow world, or an imitation world. Spiritual world is a real world. This is an imitation world. Imitation means the concepts are taken from the spiritual world. But using those concepts, an imitation world is created. For example, you know in our childhood, I am sure most of us would have played this game of father and mother. We will harass the father that no we want, we want to play father mother game. So the father would give a mini world. He will give a little house, there is a little kitchen, little little vessels, and then there is a train. And if there are some people are rich, they will have aeroplanes. All imitation. Using the concepts which are there in the regular world. The concepts are same, but the actual world that is given to the child is an imitation world, a tiny imitation world.

---
### material world, spiritual world, Mahat Tattva, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2FFahOBMS8c?t=683
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna has created original matter, that is called Mahatatva."
**Summary:** Continuing the analogy, the speaker explains that Krishna created the material world with an imitation material called Mahat Tattva to fulfill the desires of living entities who want to play the role of Krishna.

So for example, the train is there, and the train is there, and the train is there. It goes into a thing and then it comes onto the platform. So the train is there, but actually it is a tiny toy. It's not the real train. Everybody knows it's just an imitation. So similarly, this material world is also made up of some material in order to imitate the spiritual world. For example, the train is there, the children's toys are all made up of, say a big house is given, you know the child has a big house, but that is made up of some plastic. Father's house is made up of concrete, cement, permanent, or at least according to them. So similarly, for the child's play, Krishna has created original matter. Krishna has created original matter, that is called Mahatatva. He created original matter. It's not the matter which is available. There is no matter there in the spiritual world which is not the thing which is there in the spiritual world. In spiritual world everything is made up of Chintamani, Chintamani, Prakara Sadmasu, Kalpavruksha. So everything is, the houses are built on Chintamani, bricks, bricks of Chintamani. They are all conscious. We can say conscious matter. So they are all conscious, but when Krishna is trying to fulfill the child's desires, the living entities who want to play the role of Krishna, father-mother game, then for them Krishna has created this world, but he has not created with Chintamani, Prakara Sadmasu. He has not used the same material. He has created an imitation material, a shadow material, called Mahat Tattva. Here you can see Mahat Tattva is the shadow of pure consciousness. In spiritual world everything is made up of consciousness, but this world Krishna has created a shadow of that consciousness. It's a different material, appears to be matter.

---
### Mahat Tattva, elements, destiny, impermanence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2FFahOBMS8c?t=837
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna has decided that this world should not be permanent."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Mahat Tattva further divides into 24 elements, allowing for diverse manifestations according to destiny. He emphasizes that Krishna designed the material world to be temporary, like a child's sandcastle on the beach.

But the whole thing has been constructed. We will see in the next verses how that Mahat Tattva further divides itself into 24 elements, and then we have varieties to manifest whatever we want, of course according to destiny. So coming back to, so this is an important thing. Krishna has by design decided that this world should not be permanent. By design. For example, parents go to beach and children they want to play, they want to, they are playing always, and they construct house in the sand. And everything they construct, the parents never want that to be permanent. After all he has to go to the school, he has to grow, he has to do so many things. The parents know this is all child's play, it has to be temporary. This is the first design principle Krishna has decided that this child's play should end, should have a beginning and should have an end. So towards that end he has created a certain material, a original matter, a pure consciousness. Because pure consciousness is eternal. Everything in the spiritual world is eternal, permanent.

---
### Mahat Tattva, intellect, Kshetra, body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2FFahOBMS8c?t=947
**Verse References:** BG 13.1-2
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mahatatva is the total consciousness because a portion of it is represented in everyone as the intellect."
**Summary:** The speaker connects Mahat Tattva to the intellect and introduces Arjuna's question in the Bhagavad Gita about Kshetra (the field of activity), which Krishna identifies as the body.

Okay so Prabhupada is saying interestingly this Mahatma is the total consciousness. A portion of it is represented in everyone as the intellect. Now let us see how to understand this. In the 13th chapter, Bhagavad Gita 13th chapter, Arjuna asks this question. He says, Kshetra, Prakrutim purusham chaiva, Kshetram kshetradnyamevacha, Etad veditum ichyami. Arjuna says I want to understand Kshetram, the field of activity. I want to understand, Veditum ichyami, I want to know. Now Kshetra means field of activity for the living entity. The field of activity is the body. Krishna straight away answers that. He says, Shri Bhagavanu Vacha idham shariram kaunteya Kshetram idhyabhidhyate. He says what is this Kshetra? Idham shariram, this body is the Kshetra.

---
### body, elements, field of activity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2FFahOBMS8c?t=1033
**Verse References:** BG 13.5-6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This body has these elements."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna describes the body as containing specific elements, including the five great elements, false ego, intelligence, and an unmanifested element.

Now this body contains what? According to modern anatomical studies, it is been described in a certain way, some blood, urine, mucus. Ayurveda describes in a certain way. But here Krishna explains that this body has these elements. What are they? Mahabhutanahankaro Buddhiravyaktamevacha Indriyani dashayikamcha Pancha chenam Indriyagocharai. Is explaining what is this field of activity. See field of activity will refer to two things. One is this body itself is field of activity and also this entire world is also field of activity. You take any field of activity, they are made up of these 24 elements. So whether you take universal body or our body, both are made up of these 24 elements.

---
### elements, unmanifest matter, Mahat Tattva, intellect
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2FFahOBMS8c?t=1109
**Verse References:** BG 13.5-6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A little piece of Mahatatva is there in each and every body."
**Summary:** The speaker lists the 24 elements that make up the body and emphasizes the presence of an unmanifest matter, which he identifies as Mahat Tattva, suggesting that a small portion of Mahat Tattva exists in every body.

And what are they? Mahabhuthani, the great elements, the five great elements. Then Ahankara, false ego. Buddhi, intelligence. Avyaktamevacha. Now here is the secret. Krishna is saying there is something called unmanifested element is also present in the body. Avyaktamevacha. Indriyani dashayikamcha. There are 10 senses. And then pancha Indriyagochara. There are 5 sense objects. So 10 senses, 5 sense objects, 15. And then Ahankara, Buddhi, that is, and then the mind, that is 3. 15 plus 3 is 18, 25, 23, Avyaktam. 24th element is Avyaktam. So what Krishna is saying is in our body also there is an unmanifest matter. That means in this body everything cannot be understood. You can understand manifest matter by your senses. You can understand manifest matter. But Krishna is revealing here that there is unmanifest matter also. And what is the unmanifest matter? That is Mahatatva. That means a little piece of Mahatatva is there in each and every body. There is a little piece. That's what Prabhupada is saying here in the purport. Mahatatva is the total consciousness because a portion of it, a little portion of Mahatatva is represented in everyone. It is present in everyone as intellect.

---
### Kshetra, Mahat Tattva, intelligence, Pradhana, subconscious
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2FFahOBMS8c?t=1233
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Pradhana is the original form of the matter."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the presence of unmanifest matter (Mahat Tattva) in the Kshetra and connects it to intelligence. He distinguishes between Mahat Tattva and Pradhana, explaining that Pradhana is the original form of matter, while Mahat Tattva is the visible form of the three modes of nature. He also introduces the concept of contaminated consciousness or the subconscious.

Of course we understand that last portion. So what is the important thing is this Kshetra also contains unmanifest matter. Mahatatva is there. A little piece of it is there. And in the purport Prabhupada is saying here consciousness is represented by intelligence of which the unmanifest stage is the three modes of material nature. The unmanifested three modes of material nature is called Pradhana. Here also Mahatatva or Pradhana, the difference is that Pradhana is the original form of the matter. And if the three modes are visible then it is called Mahatatva. But it is still the original matter. So Prabhupada is saying here it is represented by intelligence. Here also you can see here he is saying it is represented in everyone as the intellect. So if the Mahatatva is represented by intelligence then one more element is called intelligence, Buddhi, Kam mano buddhi revacha, Manaha buddhi. So this dilemma is resolved in the third canto. Lord Kapila explains that the subtle body contains mind, intelligence and ego which we all know. But he also adds one more. He says subtle body contains consciousness and contaminated consciousness. That means what we know as the subconscious is actually the contaminated consciousness. The subconscious portion of the subtle body, mind, intelligence and ego, they have a subconscious portion and that portion is called the contaminated consciousness or Satva or the unmanifest matter. So it is a fact that there is a subconscious matter is there that is also matter only but it is unmanifest.

---

That's why you will see modern scientists are unable to figure out what this subconscious is. Although they know, they try to manipulate some kind of hypnotism. And there are some methodologies in order to manipulate this subconscious mind, intelligence and ego. So mainly it is represented as the subconscious portion of the intellect. That's what Prabhupada is saying in everyone as the intellect. So Mahatatva is the original matter. Already the three departments are evolving. We will see in the next verses each department will manifest their own parts and everything put together will be 24. From goodness certain things comes out for that department. For example mind comes from goodness. So we will see all that. So everything put together is 24. But this is the original. So this Mahatatva which is present in everyone, particularly as the subconscious portion, it records all our conscious activities. Everything that we think, our vasanas, our desires, our activities will be recorded by this little piece. It's like a chip. You know a little chip is kept in each robo. We are all caught up in this robo. It's like a little chip and it records all your actions, your desires everything it's recording. So it's a conscious matter in the sense shadow consciousness. It records all the conscious things, whatever you are doing, whatever you are thinking, where you are trying to get your happiness.

## Srimad Bhagavatam I Sri Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa I SB 3.6.22 I 18.11.20201
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

### laws of nature, scientific inquiry, origin
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FCfBINHoDpw?t=395
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Newton laws are nothing but they are about the movement, so Newton is he has not created anything he just discovered."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the example of scientists investigating the origins of the COVID-19 pandemic and Newton's discovery of gravity to illustrate how laws and rules are inherent in the universe and are simply discovered, not created.

For example, as soon as the corona pandemic came up, the first thing they were trying to understand is how this pandemic came into existence, what exactly happened. They wanted to figure out and in such pandemics, the first question is who is the first person. So they all went to China to figure out who is the first person who reported this because you know they want to understand how the origin is, how exactly it started, what did that first person do which has created this kind of a virus or pandemic. They are interested in that. So why is that they are trying to figure out all this? Because they want to see what exactly is the mechanism, what is the laws which are guiding them. Okay, so here we can see that, you know, when the whole structure for the universe has been kept in place, along with that the rules or the laws are also been thought of. So as soon as those rules are clear, then the living entities have an opportunity to travel physically. Actually, we can see the physical laws which control the movement are being defined discovered by the Newton. Newton laws are nothing but they are about the movement. So Newton is he has not created anything he just discovered. He happened to sit under the tree of an apple tree and suddenly he saw one apple falling down. He happened to ask a question why is it falling down, why it is not moving up. So that's when he started, you know, getting into the details to figure out what exactly is this phenomenon, why everything falls down. If you throw anything again it falls down. So similarly you know of course he figured out some gravitational laws, but he also pointed about the movement, the laws which are connecting to the movement.

---
### dependence, laws of nature, suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FCfBINHoDpw?t=567
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhagavatam is repeatedly pointing it out that a living entity is not all in all, a living entity is not independent, it is always dependent."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that just as traffic laws govern movement on roads, and biological laws govern walking, similarly, all aspects of life are governed by laws of nature, emphasizing the dependence of living entities.

Which are working on the roads, you cannot have a smooth ride, you cannot have a smooth life on the roads at least if you're back to the house that's a different issue on the roads you cannot have why because the many laws which are guiding the ride on the roads it is as simple so similarly when you want to walk also you can see there is a big department created here legs and then those there are rules for that there are laws for that so Bhagavatam is repeatedly pointing it out that a living entity is not all in all, a living entity is not independent, it is always dependent. It is dependent on someone even for it's just walking few bits from here to there for example sometimes when when we walk and then we don't we don't see somethings carefully then we can topple down that's one of the rule you know if you're walking walking and then this you hit something tuck you'll topple off right sometimes people break their nose and face all those things because there are rules guiding in the moment knee pains leg pains these are all nothing but the various laws of nature which are controlling this particular parts and the legs. Everyday's knee pains and knee surgeries they become very rampant because the human society has lost vision of what is law of material nature are.

### ultimate goal, Krishna consciousness, mind
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FCfBINHoDpw?t=690
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As long as the mind doesn't have a destination, then it will be disturbed, any disturbance in the mind, means, that means, there is no a proper destination defined."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts understanding the laws governing the body with the higher purpose of life, which is to reach Vishnu, emphasizing that the mind needs a final destination to avoid agitation, and that destination is Krishna.

Now Bhagavatam takes us to one more level of thinking it is fine okay you understood all these laws very nicely now you know how to what are the laws which are guiding your legs our hands or whatever the entire body you may be knowing all. Vishnu, it is talking about a different gati, there is a different destination, okay, for physical destination to reach, legs are there, there are some laws are there, yes, you can reach, but then, there is a different destination, that the shastra is talking about, and that is, the destination of reaching Vishnu, nate viduhu. So, the Bhagavatam is bringing the this focus on a different goal, what it says is, we should have two types of goals, one goal could be the immediate goal, walking from here to ashram, or walking from here to some accommodation, or some exit gate, or whatever, that could be one of the goals, or walking to our company, reaching our seat, and there also there are some destinations defined, there are some targets, so, reaching those targets, there are so many destinations we keep achieving, every day, but shastra is saying, you don't be satisfied with that, there is one more destination that you have to think of, a overarching, overarching, a life's destination, destination for the whole life, and that is gati, Vishnu, that is reaching Krishna, or Vishnu, so, that is the ultimate goal, says, as long as the mind doesn't have a destination, then, it will be disturbed, any disturbance in the mind, means, that means, there is no a proper destination defined, so, in search of that destination, in want of that proper, final destination, mind agitates, continuously agitates, and there won't be any satisfaction, so, that's why, it is important that, the mind has to be given a final destination, a proper destination.

### prasadam, transformation, real-life example
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FCfBINHoDpw?t=1311
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "They ate that food for say about some ten days and immediately you can see some change in them, this is the difference."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a real-life experience of a 'chaddi gang' (a group of ruffians) who regularly caused trouble at a prasadam canteen, but after consistently eating the prasadam for about ten days, their behavior transformed, and they became peaceful and orderly.

So then, because there is a system, you know, there is a government put system that they they have to stand in a queue, and everybody has to be peaceful, they can they have to pay, initially one or two cases they have not paid also, and then, such a chaddi gang, so then, after two three times when they came, maybe a weeks or weeks time, and then, slowly, we stopped getting complaints from the the manager, and one day our devotees went there to see what is happening, whether manager is still survived, surviving, or is gone, so, we went there to see, and, that time, the manager was telling, yes, even now, the chaddi gang is coming, every day, but now, they are not creating any trouble, and then, somebody was showing the devotees, see, this is the chaddi gang, and, they are very peacefully standing in the queue, they are all coming, five rupees, they are paying, they are taking food, they are eating, they are going, so, now, what exactly happened, it's not that we call we call police, or we put some pressure, nothing, they ate that food for say about some ten days and immediately you can see some change in them, this is the difference, they don't understand this, they think food is food, why it has to be offered in a sacrifice.

## Srimad Bhagavatam I Sri Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa I SB 3.6.34 I 09.03.2022
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

### purification, modes of nature, spiritual identity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-Reuhncefm8?t=393
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As a spirit soul we don't like these conditions, we want to be free, we want to be powerful, we want to do whatever we want to."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the concept of purification (atma vishuddhi) as freedom from the influence of the three modes of nature, highlighting the spirit soul's inherent desire for freedom and the tendency to exploit resources for bodily comfort.

Artham, a purification, one has to purify. Now what is this purification? The shastra always talks about atma vishuddhi. What is this purification? So Prabhupada is mentioned here, the purification is the purification of the impact of the three modes, purification of the influence of the three modes. The basic idea is that we are atma, here it is said atma vishuddhi, we are all actually spirit souls, but then there is a contamination. We are under some conditions, the contamination means these three modes, and the three modes will impose conditions on us. But as a spirit soul we don't like these conditions, we want to be free, we want to be powerful, we want to do whatever we want to. And one of the influence of these three modes is that one wants to exploit the resources for his own sense enjoyment. The basic thing that he thinks is, I am this body, let me exploit everything for body's comfort. Always tries to keep the body very comfortable, little bit of discomfort he cannot tolerate, and little bit of pain, little bit of suffering he doesn't want to tolerate. This is the mood of a living entity. In this world everybody is running behind how they can enhance their enjoyment. This is how the body can be. Atma vishuddhi, we are all actually spirit souls. Atma vishuddhi, we are all actually spirit souls. Atma vishuddhi, we are all actually spirit souls.

### varnashrama, brahmanical culture, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-Reuhncefm8?t=529
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Brahmanical culture culminating in transcendental service of the Lord is the basic principle of purifying the soul."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of brahmanical culture and the varnashrama system, highlighting the role of the brahmana as a spiritual master and the ultimate goal of transcendental service to the Lord for purifying the soul.

In society everybody is simply running behind how to enhance, how to earn more and more money. But actually Lord has created these varnas: brahmana, kshatriya, vaishya, and shudra. These varnas and also He has defined what is the swadharma for the sake of increasing culture, enhancing the culture. And what kind of a culture is that? It is called here brahmanical culture. So here it is said the brahmana is the spiritual master of all the orders of the society and thus brahmanical culture culminating in transcendental service of the Lord is the basic principle of purifying the soul. Everybody is a spiritual master of all the orders of the society. Every sentence is shown as purifying the soul. Now the souls are having this tendency to lord it over and Prabhupada is saying the last snare or the last goal of this tendency is to become God himself. One claims that he is supreme. Prabhupada says how one can be supreme if he is under the contamination? He cannot be the supreme. So this brahmanical culture will help everybody. So what is this brahmanical culture? First of all this division is there. The four divisions should be there: brahmana, kshatriya, vaishya, and shudra. And they all have to accept the brahmana as the spiritual master. All the varnas and all the ashramas are there. So this brahmana culture will help everybody have to work under the direction of the spiritual master, swagurum harim. And they have to worship the Supreme Lord Hari under the direction of the spiritual master. And then what will the brahmana tell? He says the division of the society is not meant for enhancing the bodily comforts but rather to culture the soul, enhance the soul's comfort. This is the difference. Brahmana means one who knows that there is a spirit soul and he knows that the spirit soul has its own comfort. He knows the constitution of the spirit soul. He is called brahmana. Somebody who knows medicine he is called a doctor. Similarly somebody who knows about the soul and its…

Creating facilities will not save you from this contamination, will not save you from this conditions of life. So Western civilization means simply they enhance the money, money, money, in order to cover up the defects of the culture. Big money, lighting, all facilities and machines and this and that. But such a civilization cannot exist. Culture has to be there at the family level, culture has to be there at the civil level. What is culture at the family level? The man, he works honestly to his best capacity and he brings money to the house and the wife, very intelligently, whatever money comes, very intelligently she manages the house. She doesn't complain about how much money has been brought to the house. She will not complain. As soon as the man comes, she will not harass. Why only this much? I want more. Where is my abaran? Where is my saree? Where is my one more cupboard? She rather, whatever money comes, by the honest endeavor of the man, she takes that and very intelligently she manages the family. Even now we see some families, families like that, wife will never complain. She has great respect for the husband and for his hard endeavor and whatever comes, she very expertly utilizes for running the family.

They are not interested in Swagurum Harim, how to worship Hari, so that their conditions will come down. They can minimize and simplify their lives. They may have, say, half a dozen cars. You go and ask, can you reduce one car? How dare you are asking me to reduce one car? It is my social symbol, my prestige, you know.
