## Why Bhakta Hanuman is so Famous? | Sri Sampati Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

### servant leadership, Hanuman's qualities, dharma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xmjUwmJmQY0?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Hanuman used his power, his intelligence, his courage in the service of delivering dharma and nyaya."
**Summary:** This segment introduces Hanuman as an inspiring example of servant leadership, highlighting his power, intelligence, and courage in upholding dharma and justice.

How to become a servant leader. Ravan also had many powerful intelligent servants. Nobody is remembered today. Hanuman used his power, his intelligence, his courage in the service of delivering dharma and nyaya. Did he ever ask anything in return? What is the reward that Hanuman received? What are the reasons of Hanuman's enduring and ever-increasing popularity and worshipping Hanuman in Sanatan Dharma? Hanuman is an inspiring, motivating character in Valmiki Ramayana that teaches us how to be a good servant leader. Hanuman is considered very powerful, intelligent, courageous and very satisfied by rendering service. When somebody is powerful, intelligent, courageous, smart, they like to become a leader. But we see Hanuman being happy and satisfied being a servant leader. Hanuman is considered very powerful, intelligent, courageous, smart, they like to become a servant leader.

---
### dharma, justice, compassion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xmjUwmJmQY0?t=82
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Hanuman is popular and worshipped because throughout his life, he used his power, his intelligence, his courage in the service of Dharma, in the service of Nyaya, in the service of delivering Dharma and Nyaya."
**Summary:** This segment illustrates Hanuman's commitment to dharma and justice through examples like supporting Sugriv and rescuing Sita, emphasizing his role as a savior of the weak and distressed.

Because Hanuman stood for dharma and nyaya. Whenever there was injustice done to anyone, Hanuman was there to deliver justice. Example is Sugriv. Sugriv was actually attacked by his own brother, Vali. And Sugriv was thrown out of his own kingdom, Kishkindha. He was wronged. So Hanuman supported Sugriv. He supported weak people. This is the quality of those who are powerful. A power is always used in the service of the weak people. In that way, Hanuman was in the side of Sugriv. When Hanuman came to know about Lord Shriram, whose wife was kidnapped, that means there was injustice done to Lord Shriram by Ravana. Immediately Hanuman came to do saviour. To deliver justice to Shriram, Hanuman also worked very hard to bring justice to distressed women like Mother Sita. Sita was put into a very, very distressful condition by Ravana and Hanuman fought with great valour to give justice to Mother Sita. So Hanuman is popular and worshipped because throughout his life, he used his power, his intelligence, his courage in the service of Dharma, in the service of Nyaya, in the service of delivering Dharma and Nyaya.

---
### leadership, dharma, legacy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xmjUwmJmQY0?t=180
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Anybody who takes the side of adharma and anyai, they will be dead and they will be forgotten."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts Hanuman's enduring fame with the forgotten servants of Ravana and other historical figures like Napoleon and Hitler, highlighting the importance of aligning with dharma and serving a glorious leader like Shriram.

That is why he is very popular. Another important thing is, Hanuman is popular because his leader is a glorious leader. He became the servant of a glorious leader like Shriram. In Ramayana, we also see Ravana, who is also powerful. Ravan also had many powerful intelligent servants but nobody is remembered today. They all gave up their lives serving Ravan but nobody is worshipped and nobody, we don't even know their names because they were all on the side of adharma and the side of anyai. Anybody who takes the side of adharma and anyai, they will be dead and they will be forgotten. Even in the modern history, there are many so-called powerful people like Napoleon, like Hitler. Do we even remember them or their followers? No, we don't even know who are their followers. But Hanuman, from thousands of years, people remember, glorify, worship him because he stood for nyaya and dharma and that is why he has become an inspiration for everyone to follow.

---
### selflessness, love, servant leadership
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xmjUwmJmQY0?t=253
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Hanuman received nothing in return. He received the love of Shri Ram."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes Hanuman's selflessness and the love he received from Shri Ram as his reward, presenting him as an example of a servant leader who serves dharma and justice without expecting anything in return.

Even though Hanuman did so much service to Lord Shri Ram, did he ever ask anything in return? When I become servant of somebody, I am always thinking what is the profit of doing so much of service? What did I get in return? What is the reward that Hanuman received? Hanuman received nothing in return. He received the love of Shri Ram. So here is a selfless person. For whom, the service to dharma, service to nyaya itself is a reward. So I think we all have an inherent attraction to such people who stand up for dharma, who stand up for nyaya selflessly, not for any reward. So Hanuman is that example who selflessly fought for nyaya and dharma for Shri Ram and he remained very happy and satisfied. Servant of Shri Ram. So he teaches an example how to become a servant leader. He is a good servant of Shri Ram and he is a good leader for all of us to follow. That is why Hanuman is popular and his popularity is ever increasing and people love to worship Hanuman.

## Why Great Souls Surrender to Krishna? | SB 3.24.32 | HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

### surrender, opulence, Absolute Truth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T-rAHNh1IxY?t=0
**Verse References:** BG 9.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you want to be perfect, just always think of Me, become My devotee, worship Me and offer your obeisances to Me."
**Summary:** The speaker reads and explains a verse from Srimad Bhagavatam and a related verse from Bhagavad Gita, emphasizing the importance of surrendering to Krishna, who is full of six opulences.

Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 3, Chapter 24, and Text 32. Uribihi, Tatva, Bubutsa, Ayaddha, Sadha, Abhivadha, Arhana, Padapeetam, Aishwarya, Vairagya, Yashaha, Avabhodha, Dheerya, Shriya, Portham, Aham, Prapadhey. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada. My dear Lord, Your lotus feet are the reservoir that always deserves to receive worshipful homage from all great sages eager to understand the Absolute Truth. You are full in opulence, renunciation, transcendental fame, knowledge, strength and beauty and therefore I surrender myself unto Your lotus feet. Purport by Srila Prabhupada. Actually, those who are searching after the Absolute Truth must take shelter of the lotus feet of the Supreme Personality of Godhead and worship Him. In Bhagavad Gita, Lord Krishna advised Arjuna many times to surrender unto Him, especially at the end of 9th Chapter, Manmana Bhavamad Bhakta. If you want to be perfect, just always think of Me, become My devotee, worship Me and offer your obeisances to Me. In this way, you will understand Me, the Personality of Godhead, and ultimately you will come back to Me, back to Godhead, back home. Why is it so? The Lord is always full in six opulences, as mentioned herein: wealth, renunciation, fame, knowledge, strength and beauty. He is full in everything.

---
### opulence, Bhagavan, Absolute Truth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T-rAHNh1IxY?t=163
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If anybody wants to understand Absolute Truth, then they are having a desire to understand Tattva, Tattva means the Absolute Truth, the Ultimate Transcendental Knowledge, then they have to offer their worshipable homage unto your Lotus Feet."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Krishna's six opulences, explaining the meaning of 'Purtam' and 'Asamavoodhva'. He connects these concepts to the definition of Bhagavan and emphasizes the necessity of offering worship to the Lord's lotus feet to understand the Absolute Truth.

And no one can surpass Him. Another one of Krishna's names is Asamavoodhva, which means that no one is equal to or greater than Him. So, Kardama Muni is offering his prayers to Lord Vishnu, and in this verse he says, Aham Prapadhye, I surrender unto you. He says the Lord is full of Purtam, He is full of Aishvarya, that is the wealth, Vairagya, the renunciation, Yashah, transcendental fame, Avabhodha, knowledge, Virya, the strength, and Shriya, beauty. So, the six opulences are listed here in this sloka. A same list is given by Parashara Muni also, explaining how Bhagavan is defined as the one who has all these opulences in full, in infinite quantity, that is known as Bhagavan. And so here, he is saying, if anybody wants to understand Absolute Truth, then, Tatva Bubusyada, they are having a desire to understand Tattva. Tattva means the Absolute Truth, the Ultimate Transcendental Knowledge, then, Twam Suri Bi Sadha Bi Arahana Padapitam, then, they have to offer their worshipable homage unto your Lotus Feet.

### worship, knowledge, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T-rAHNh1IxY?t=1030
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Anybody who understands that the Supreme Lord is full of all opulences, he is a original personality, he is a original source and worships him."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that worshiping the lotus feet of the Lord leads to knowledge, contrasting this with simply hearing or engaging in conversation. ### devotional service, understanding, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T-rAHNh1IxY?t=1378
**Verse References:** BG 10.10
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I give the understanding by which they can come to me."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts discussing Krishna as Maya with directly worshipping Him. He quotes Krishna from the Bhagavad Gita, stating that those constantly devoted and worshipping with love will be given the understanding to return to Him, emphasizing Krishna's responsibility in providing knowledge to surrendered devotees.

There is no point discussing Krishna as Maya, Vishnu Maya. There are so many such podcasts and interviews will happen and they will all be giving various ideas, how you can become big in this, how you can become good in this, ultimately, it will not work. But just by worshipping and you know, being part of the being, basically, you cannot become You know, just by serving Krishna, everything will happen. That's what Krishna is saying. Tesham satatayuktanam bhajatam prithipurvakam. Satatayuktanam, if a devotee is always engaged, those who are constantly devoted and worship me with love, let us say a pujari is there, he is only worshipping, but he is constantly, satatayuktanam, constantly engaged. 24 hours a devotee is engaged in Krishna's service. That is pure devotee. 24 hours thinking, not that, okay, I can think now up to some level, then I can give one hour for politics, and another one more hour for some other thing, then I will be alright. No, Krishna here is saying, Tesham satatayuktanam, satatam means always, bhajatam prithipurvakam, with love they render service to Krishna, then Krishna is saying, dadhami buddhi yogam tam enamam upayantite, I give the understanding by which they can come to me. Now Krishna is taking responsibility of giving the understanding. Giving the knowledge. So, the question, that if I simply worship Krishna everything will happen? Yes, Krishna is saying, yes, I will give the understanding by which they can come to me. You can go back to Krishna only when you become perfect in absolute knowledge, absolute truth. That's what Kaddamuni is saying, tattva bhavasa, one who wants to understand absolute truth, better they worship the Buddha. And he himself is saying, aham prapadhey, let me surrender. Now Krishna is saying, if somebody surrenders to me and renders services, what will happen? I give the understanding by which he can come to me.

### compassion, knowledge, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T-rAHNh1IxY?t=1534
**Verse References:** BG 10.11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The sincere devotee engaged in Krishna consciousness, cannot be without knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes another verse from Bhagavad Gita where Krishna promises to destroy the darkness of ignorance with the lamp of knowledge for those who worship Him. He then shares an anecdote about Lord Chaitanya and Prakashananda Saraswati, emphasizing that sincere devotees are helped by Krishna Himself within their hearts, regardless of their philosophical understanding.

Then final shloka he says, Tesham evanukam paddham, aham ajnanajam tamaha, nashayame atma bhavasto, jnanadeepena bhashut. Here Krishna is saying, out of compassion for them, for the devotees who are worshipping, for them, dwelling in their hearts, I dwelling in their hearts, destroy with the shining lamp of knowledge, the darkness born of ignorance. So here, in our hearts there could be ignorance, darkness, but the Lord is assuring that with a lamp of knowledge, I destroy this darkness, because I am dwelling there. Hmm? Prabhupada in the Paripoti writes nicely, when Lord Chaitanya was in Benares, promulgating the chanting of Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, thousands of people were following him. Prakashananda, a very influential and learned scholar in Benares at that time, derided Lord Chaitanya for being a sentimentalist. Sometimes philosophers criticize the devotees, because they think that most of the devotees are in the darkness of ignorance, and are philosophically very naive sentimentalists. They think, okay, they are doing some Aachana, they are doing some Pooja, okay, study Pooja, you don't know Vedanta. You don't have any learning, you don't have any philosophy, like that sometimes they make a mockery of the devotees, actually that is not the fact. There are very very learned scholars who have put forward the philosophy of devotion, but even if a devotee does not take advantage of their literatures, or of his spiritual master, let us say spiritual master has written a lot of books, and then the devotee is not able to take advantage, seriously, if a devotee does not take advantage of the literature given by his spiritual master, if he is sincere in his devotional service, if he is very sincerely doing every day, he is helped by Krishna himself within his heart. This is the secret of a devotee's knowledge, his philosophy, and the capacity to understand the philosophy. This is the secret. Krishna, he takes the responsibility. He says, I destroy the darkness in their hearts. So the sincere devotee engaged in Krishna consciousness, cannot be without knowledge.

### Krishna consciousness, Vedanta, chanting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T-rAHNh1IxY?t=1728
**Verse References:** BG 15.15
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you want to summarize Vedanta in one word, that is Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker shares an example of a young boy repeating philosophical truths, emphasizing that the only qualification is devotional service in full Krishna consciousness. He concludes by stating that devotees are mystical personalities, and that chanting Hare Krishna is the perfection of Vedanta, summarizing everything in Krishna.

A sincere devotee who is engaged in Krishna consciousness, he cannot be without knowledge. You see small small children I am seeing, if at all you want to see, see their podcast. Little little children, and then they are asking, how do we live our life? And he is answering. He is this much height, that boy is. Without getting affected by victory or loss, you should completely surrender to Krishna and perfect your life. You know, he is just repeating whatever he has heard, without getting disturbed by the dualities of this world, by your prescribed duties, you worship Krishna and attain perfection. This is what he said and everybody is shocked. So, the only qualification is that, one carry out devotional service in full Krishna consciousness. This is the only qualification. Carry out devotional service in full Krishna consciousness. Actually, devotees are the most mystical, mystical personalities. Their process is completely mystical and transcendental. They look very simple, they just go on, they may be doing some ordinary things, cleaning, mopping and all that, but if you touch them and ask them one question, you will see perfect Vedanta knowledge coming out from their mouths. Even if you think that he doesn't know, he will certainly say, chant Hare Krishna. Whoever is asking, he will say, chant Hare Krishna. Hare Krishna and that is the perfection of Vedanta. If you want to summarize Vedanta in one word, that is Krishna. Vedanta, if you want to summarize in just only one word, that is nothing but Krishna. Krishna summarizes everything. Vedaishya sarvaya aham eva vedyaha, Krishna himself is saying in Bhagavad Gita, by Vedanta everybody is seeking me, searching after me, this is Krishna. So, if the devotee just says, chant Hare Krishna, he is giving full Vedanta knowledge to him. Now what else you want? So that's why, never think that this devotional service, is something less or something inferior to the so called Vedanta and philosophy.

## Why did Lord Ram banish Sita to forest? | Sri Sampati Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

### leadership, character, public perception
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJD7aOfE0tI?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is very important to have spotless character to continue to be king and queen."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the question of why Lord Ram banished Sita, emphasizing the importance of spotless character for leaders and addressing potential doubts about Sita's fitness as queen.

Did Lord Shri Ram doubt his beloved wife, Mother Sita, especially after she gave Agni Pariksha? Today, we all demand that our leaders should be corruption free. If there are any allegations of corruption, we ask them to resign from your post, just because an ordinary citizen passed in your comments, they had this doubt that whether she is fit. Oh, look how Ram has become so blindly attached to his wife. He is so lusty. It is very important to have spotless character to continue to be king and queen.

---
### dharma, leadership, morality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJD7aOfE0tI?t=36
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It was very important for king and queen to have spotless character and that inspires the citizen also to follow the dharma marga, the righteous path."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Lord Ram never doubted Sita, but banished her due to public perception and the importance of a king and queen maintaining spotless character to inspire citizens to follow dharma.

Did Lord Shri Ram doubt his beloved wife, Mother Sita, especially after she gave Agni Pariksha? Just because an ordinary citizen passed in your comments on Sita's character? No, never. Lord Shri Ram never doubted his wife, Sita. Then how do we understand? Then why did he send Mother Sita to forest just because of somebody doubting the character of Sita? It is because we need to understand Lord Shri Ram and Sita, they are not just husband and wife. They are also the king and queen of Ayodhya. As a king and queen of Ayodhya, it is very important to have spotless character to continue to be king and queen. So when the citizens had this doubt that their queen was with some other man for one year, they had this doubt that whether she is fit to continue to be queen of Ayodhya. Like today, we all demand that our leaders should be corruption free. If we come to know or if we get a doubt that this leader is involved in some gotala, there is some corruption charges, we many times we see people asking them to step down. We ask them resign from your post because you know you have to prove that you are corruption free. If there are any allegations of corruption, you cannot continue to occupy the post of a minister. Similarly, in those days, it is not just corruption, it was also character which was more important, right. So it was very important for king and queen to have spotless character and that inspires the citizen also to follow the dharma marga, the righteous path. If the king and queen themselves have a dubious character, compromised character, immoral, unethical, that will encourage the citizens also to follow adharma.

---
### dharma, leadership, sacrifice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJD7aOfE0tI?t=177
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So Lord Ram and Mother Sita were teaching that to their citizens by themselves having spotless character."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that leaders must inspire ethical behavior and that Lord Ram distanced Sita from the throne to uphold dharma, while maintaining his personal relationship with her.

In their life, so it is not just enough for the king and queen to do economic development and give all the facilities in the citizen, there also should be inspiring figures to follow dharma, to follow morality, to follow ethical good behavior, conduct. So Lord Ram and Mother Sita were teaching that to their citizens by themselves having spotless character. So Mother Sita, as a queen, her character was doubted. That is why Lord Sri Ram, till her character was proven again, asked Mother Sita to be away from the politics of Ayodhya or away from the palace, away from the king's life. As a king, he distanced himself from the queen. As husband, he always maintained his highest relationship with Mother Sita. He never doubted Mother Sita's character.

---
### devotion, leadership, sacrifice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJD7aOfE0tI?t=231
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Rama gave the glorious position of wife to mother Sita only, not to anyone else, not that he rejected Sita."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the example of Rajasuya Yagna to illustrate that Lord Ram never doubted Sita's character as his wife, even while distancing her as queen due to public doubt.

When Ram did Rajasuya Yagna, Rajasuya Yagna or any Yagna can always be done by a married person with his wife, because wife has equal rights to participate in Yagna. At that time, Sita was at the forest. As a ideal king, Lord Sri Ram, distanced himself from Queen Sita, whose character was under question. But, as a loving husband he never doubted his beloved wife Sita. Rajasuya is an example for this. Rajasuya Yajna was done by kings and when Lord Shri Ram was about to do Rajasuya Yajna, the priest in Ayodhya questioned Lord Shri Ram, where is the wife? Because without wife, you cannot perform Rajasuya Yajna alone. At that time, Lord Shri Ram as loving husband got golden statue of Sita, his beloved wife and along with that golden statue Sita, he completed Rajasuya Yajna. If Lord Ram had any doubt about Sita's character, why would he do golden statue of his wife Sita? He gave that glorious position of his wife. In the Rajasuya Yajna, he said that only Sita is eligible to be part of Rajasuya Yajna as my wife. He never rejected Sita as his beloved wife. Yes, queen whose character was under doubt, the public had the doubt, not the king Ram, husband Ram especially. So Rajasuya Yajna episode tells us Rama gave the glorious position of wife to mother Sita only, not to Sita.

---
### leadership, sacrifice, nationalism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJD7aOfE0tI?t=349
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Lord Sri Ram is very different leader. He chose nation. He chose the growth of the country first. He chose the citizens' comfort first, not his personal life."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts Lord Ram's leadership with modern examples of leaders prioritizing family over country, highlighting Ram's sacrifice and focus on the welfare of his citizens.

Not to anyone else, not that he rejected Sita. And another very important lesson that many people miss out is the topic of Parivarvaat, today we hear. Today we hear how leaders, powerful people and those occupy the leadership and powerful position in our country do everything for their own family, for their family members, right? At the cost of their own family members, they do everything for their own family members. At the cost of citizens at the cost of the growth of the country. They're very happy to see their family growing, the family property growing, and being with family, being together family, they say, no, no, no, I can't be in the jail. I want to be with my wife. I want to be with my children. But what about country's growth? Nobody cares. But Lord Sri Ram is very different leader. He chose nation. He chose the growth of the country first. He chose the citizens' comfort first, not his personal life. He kept his personal life away from the country. Where do we find such great leaders today? Ram is an inspiration for all the leaders.

---
### leadership, justice, public opinion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJD7aOfE0tI?t=427
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "He said in his kingdom, citizens can without any fear even question the credibility of the king and queen."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts Lord Ram's tolerance of criticism with authoritarian regimes, emphasizing that Ram allowed citizens to question even the king and queen, and that he didn't use his power to silence dissent.

Today, it is very hard to find people who keep their family away and country first. Today, sometimes we hear like in the example of Bangkok in Thailand, there is a monarch dynasty. If you pass any comments, negative comments on the king or that dynasty, you will be jailed. You will be sent to jail. In fact, you may be sentenced. The life sentence be given for those who talk against the royal family. Lord Ram was a monarch. He had all the power in his hand. He could have immediately won. He could have immediately executed those who commented on mother Sita. But he didn't punish those who passed comments on the Sita. He said in his kingdom, citizens can without any fear even question the credibility of the king and queen. He did not threaten the citizen. He did not scare the citizen. I am the powerful person. How dare you talk on my family members? He was okay to be questioned. A king also can be questioned, he said. For that, he could have easily taken the side of Sita. And he said, no, I know the character of my wife Sita. He did not appear to be Parivarwadi. He said, let other authorities justify the Sita's character. Like today, we go to Supreme Court. If there is any corruption charge, the government themselves cannot say we are clean. We are clean. If there is any allegation. A third party like Supreme Court should hear and give verdict. And once the Supreme Court gives verdict, everybody will accept it. If Ram himself speaks in favour of Sita, then people will say, Oh, look how Ram has become so blindly attached to his wife. He obviously speaks in favour of his wife. He is so lusty. He is attached to his wife. So Ram did not take the side of mother Sita. So he distanced himself from the queen.

---
### authority, validation, purity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJD7aOfE0tI?t=552
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Valmiki gave his verdict that Sita is, she has spotless character."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Valmiki, a respected sage, vouched for Sita's character, similar to a Supreme Court verdict, which allowed Ram to welcome her back as queen.

But as husband, he always gave the position of a great wife to mother Sita. Later, in another authority in the society, in Treta Yuga, the great sages were given the great authority. Like the kings, they were also authorities. When Valmiki comes and he says with his tapas shakti of 60,000 years of tapasya, he says, I vouch for the character of Sita. And everybody now accepts. He accepts the statement of Valmiki because Valmiki does not have any favouritism, right? Because Sita is no way connected to Valmiki. And Valmiki, who was so pure with his tapas shakti, who has that faith and trust in the public, the public was ready to accept the statement of Valmiki. And when he said that Sita is clean, Sita's character is pure, then Ram said she can come back as the queen of Ayodhya. So, like Supreme Court gave verdict, Valmiki gave his verdict that Sita is, she has spotless character.

### devotion, dharma, leadership
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJD7aOfE0tI?t=621
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Sri Ram is being away from such loving wife, just to show to the people, the whole world that how a leader always chooses his duty first, country first, welfare of the citizen first, and family is last."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing Sita's devotion to Ram, her understanding of his dharma, and Ram's ultimate prioritization of duty, country, and the welfare of citizens over personal happiness, making him a true nationalistic leader.

At that time, Ram was ready to welcome mother Sita. If at all Ram mistreated his wife Sita, he doubted the character of Sita, he has hurt the feelings and emotions of Sita, then what was Sita's response to this? The Sita's response itself will tell us, that how she was happy with Sri Ram's action of sending her to forest. At the end, mother Sita asked that life after life, I want Sri Ram as my husband. If Sri Ram mistreated her, why she wants Sri Ram again as her husband? That itself will tell us, clarify all the doubts, as a responsible, mature queen, Sita understood the Dharma Sankat of Ram. Ram has dual roles to play. He has a role of a king, role of a husband. So, the family of Lord Sri Ram always stood by country, stood by the welfare of the citizen, not the personal happiness, personal benefit. When it was about to do Pattabhisheka, become king, at that time also Ram chose Ayodhya first, his personal happiness later. He went to forest in order to demonstrate the entire citizen, here is a government that always keeps up the promise. It never goes away from the words, the promises that we have made, Raghukula Reethi Sada Chaliyai. Even at the cost of being in the forest and going through so much tribulation, even at this case, Lord Sri Ram is being away from such loving wife, just to show to the people, the whole world that how a leader always chooses his duty first, country first, welfare of the citizen first, and family is last. He is never a Parivarwadi, in fact he is a Rashtravadi. He is a great, true, inspiring, nationalistic leader that we all aspiring to have in our life.
